  still saw `invalid_request`. Grounding against the openclaw + claude-code
  source trees plus extracting the actual `h6.post` call site from
  claude.exe (`{"Content-Type":"application/json"}`, no beta header)
  confirmed the binary is the ground truth: JSON-only on the token
  endpoint, no anthropic-beta. Public docs and community gists are
  incorrect on this point.

## [0.99.2] έΑΦ 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` έΑΦ token endpoint host + Content-Type + timeout ΉιΧΉιΧ.**
  v0.99.1 manual-paste fix ΊδΕΉΩΡΈΠΕ `/login anthropic` Ά░Α `invalid_request`
  Έκε Ά▒░ΉιΙ. ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡ Ή╜αΉΗΦ ΉΜιΊα╕ + Claude Code native binary Ήζα prod env Ά░ζΉ▓┤
  `K3q` ΉιΕΉ▓┤ Ή╢ΦΉ╢ε + Ά│╡ΉΜζ Έυ╕ΉΕε cross-check Έκε 3 Ά░ΑΉπΑ root cause ΊβΧΉιΧ:
  έΣι token endpoint host Ά░Α `https://platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token`
  (`api.anthropic.com` ΉζΑ inference API ΉιΕΉγσ); έΣκ Content-Type ΉζΑ
  `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` ΈπΝ ΊΩΙΉγσ έΑΦ `application/json` Ήε╝Έκε
  Έ│┤ΈΓ┤Έσ┤ ΉζΣΈΜ╡ ΉπΑΉΩ░/timeout Ά░ΑΈΛξ; έΣλ ΉζΣΈΜ╡ ΉΜεΆ░Ε 40-60s Έ│┤Ά│ιΆ░Α ΉηΙΉΨ┤ client
  timeout ΉζΕ 15s έΗΤ 60s Έκε ΉβΕΊβΦ. `_ANTHROPIC_TOKEN_URL` ΉιΧΉιΧ + `json=` έΗΤ
  `data=` body ΊαΧΉΜζ Έ│ΑΆ▓╜ + httpx timeout 60s.

- **`login_anthropic()` έΑΦ corrected token endpoint host, Content-Type, and
  timeout.** Post-v0.99.1 the manual-paste flow still failed with
  `invalid_request`. Three root causes pinned down via the prod env object
  `K3q` extracted from Claude Code's native binary plus cross-check with
  official docs: έΣι OAuth audience lives on
  `platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token` (`api.anthropic.com` only serves
  the inference API); έΣκ endpoint accepts only
  `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` έΑΦ `application/json` causes hang/
  timeout; έΣλ reported 40-60s response time under load, so client timeout
  relaxed from 15s to 60s. Fixed `_ANTHROPIC_TOKEN_URL`, switched httpx
  body from `json=` to `data=`, set Timeout(60).

## [0.99.1] έΑΦ 2026-05-17

### Fixed

- **`login_anthropic()` έΑΦ loopback redirect_uri έΗΤ manual-paste ΊΝρΊΕ┤ Ά╡ΡΉ▓┤.**
  v0.99.0 ΉΩΡΉΕε ΈΠΕΉηΖΈΡε loopback HTTP server (`http://localhost:54123/callback`)
  ΈΛΦ OAuth client `9d1c250a-έΑο` ΉΩΡ ΈΥ▒ΈκζΈΡε redirect URI Ά░Α ΉΧΕΈΜΙΈζ╝ authorize
  ΈΜρΆ│ΕΉΩΡΉΕε Ά▒░ΉιΙΈΡΡΈΜν (ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡ Έ│┤Ά│ι έΑΦ ΈΣΡ Έ▓Ι ΉΜεΈΠΕ ΈςρΈΣΡ ~50Ή┤Ι ΈπΝΉΩΡ ΉΜνΊΝρ, auth.toml
  Έψ╕Έ│ΑΆ▓╜). Claude Code native binary Ήζα strings Έ╢ΕΉΕζΉε╝Έκε ΉιΧΈΜ╡ redirect URI
  Ά░Α `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/code/callback` ΉηΕΉζΕ ΊβΧΉζ╕ έΑΦ ΉΕεΈ▓Ε Ή╕κ
  callback ΊΟαΉζ┤ΉπΑΆ░Α ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡΉΩΡΆ▓Ν `code#state` ΊαΧΉΜζΉζΕ ΊΣεΉΜεΊΧαΈσ┤ ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡΆ░Α CLI
  Έκε paste ΊΧαΈΛΦ manual-paste ΊΝρΊΕ┤. `_run_anthropic_pkce_flow` Έξ╝ 1:1
  Έψ╕ΈθυΈκε ΉηυΉηΣΉΕ▒: HTTPServer / `_pick_free_port` / Ή╜εΈ░▒ ΊΧ╕ΈΥνΈθυ ΉιεΆ▒░, paste
  ΊΝΝΉΕε (`_parse_pasted_code` έΑΦ URL/fragment/bare code 3 ΊαΧΉΜζ ΉΙαΉγσ) ΈΠΕΉηΖ,
  scope ΉΩΡ `user:sessions:claude_code` Ή╢ΦΆ░Α (binary hint ΉιΧΊΧσ). Tier 3
  impersonation ΉιΧΉ▒ΖΉζΑ Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε.

- **`login_anthropic()` έΑΦ switched loopback redirect to manual-paste.**
  v0.99.0's loopback HTTP server (`http://localhost:54123/callback`) was
  rejected at the authorize step because the OAuth client `9d1c250a-έΑο`
  is registered with only one redirect URI:
  `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/code/callback` (server-hosted).
  Confirmed by `strings(8)` analysis of Claude Code's native binary +
  user reports of two consecutive ~50s failures with no auth.toml write.
  `_run_anthropic_pkce_flow` rewritten as a 1:1 mirror of Claude Code's
  manual-paste flow: the loopback HTTP server, port picker, and request
  handler are removed; a paste parser (`_parse_pasted_code`) accepts the
  full callback URL, the `code#state` fragment, or the bare code; scope
  set extended with `user:sessions:claude_code` per the binary's hint
  string. Tier 3 impersonation posture unchanged.

## [0.99.0] έΑΦ 2026-05-17

### Added

- **`login_anthropic()` έΑΦ owned-Anthropic OAuth PKCE flow (claude CLI
  ΉζαΉκ┤ΉΕ▒ ΉιεΆ▒░).** `/login anthropic` Ά░Α ΈΞΦ Ήζ┤ΉΔΒ `claude /login`
  subprocess Έξ╝ Ία╕Ή╢εΊΧαΉπΑ ΉΧΛΆ│ι GEODE Ά░Α ΉπΒΉιΣ PKCE redirect flow ΉΙαΊΨΚ
  έΑΦ loopback callback server (ΈηεΈΞν free port 54123-54199), PKCE
  code_verifier/challenge ΉΔζΉΕ▒, `https://platform.claude.com/oauth/
  authorize` browser open, `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token`
  ΊΗιΊΒ░ Ά╡ΡΊβα, `~/.geode/auth.toml` Ήζα `providers.anthropic` section ΉΩΡ
  ΉιΑΉηξ. multi-candidate client_id ΉΜεΈΠΕ path (`9d1c250a-...` ΈΥ▒ reverse-
  engineered) + first-success-wins. macOS/Linux/Windows ΈςρΈΣΡ ΈΠβΉηΣ.
  `read_geode_anthropic_credentials` ΊΩυΊΞ╝Ά░Α `read_geode_openai_
  credentials` ΉβΑ ΈΠβΉζ╝ shape Ήε╝Έκε Έ░αΊβα. `claude_code_provider.
  resolve_claude_oauth_token` / `get_claude_oauth_metadata` Ά░Α auth.
  toml Ήγ░ΉΕι read + macOS keychain backwards-compat fallback. ToS Tier
  3 (impersonation) έΑΦ `claude_code_provider` Ήζα module docstring Ήζα
  policy notice Ά░Α SOT. failure ΉΜε graceful fallback (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
  Ά╢ΝΉηξ message).
- **`login_anthropic()` έΑΦ owned-Anthropic OAuth PKCE flow (drops
  `claude` CLI dependency).** `/login anthropic` no longer spawns
  `claude /login`; GEODE drives the PKCE redirect flow itself έΑΦ
  loopback callback (free port 54123-54199), PKCE
  `code_verifier`/`challenge` pair, browser open against
  `platform.claude.com/oauth/authorize`, token exchange at
  `api.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token`, persist into
  `~/.geode/auth.toml` `providers.anthropic`. Multi-candidate
  `client_id` loop (`9d1c250a-...` first) with first-success-wins.
  Cross-platform (macOS / Linux / Windows). `read_geode_anthropic_
  credentials` mirrors the OpenAI helper shape.
  `claude_code_provider.resolve_claude_oauth_token` and
  `get_claude_oauth_metadata` now prefer the `auth.toml` source with
  the macOS keychain kept as a backwards-compat fallback. ToS Tier 3
  (impersonation) per `claude_code_provider` module docstring;
  failure surfaces an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` fallback hint.


- **`docs/architecture/provider-login.md` έΑΦ provider login flow SOT.**
  OpenAI (device-code) ΉβΑ Anthropic (PKCE redirect) Ήζα OAuth flow Ήζα
  ΉιΧΊΧσ spec ΉΜιΆ╖ε. owned-credential ΊΝρΊΕ┤ (auth.toml SOT + GEODE Ά░Α ΉπΒΉιΣ
  OAuth client) Ήζα architecture + 5-mismatch ΉιΧΊΧσ plan + ToS Tier
  spectrum (0-4) ΉιΧΉζα. PR C3 (owned-Anthropic PKCE Ά╡υΊαΕ) Ήζα reference
  SOT.
- **`docs/architecture/provider-login.md` έΑΦ provider login flow SOT.**
  Architecture spec for the two-provider OAuth picture: OpenAI's
  device-code flow plus Anthropic's PKCE redirect flow share an
  owned-credential pattern (auth.toml as SOT, GEODE acts as OAuth
  client directly). Covers the 5 mismatch points the upcoming PR C3
  will close and pins down the ToS Tier (0-4) spectrum the choice
  sits in.

### Removed

- **`/auth` ΉΛυΈηαΉΜε ΈςΖΈι╣ ΉβΕΉιΕ ΉιεΆ▒░ + `/login source` ΉΜιΉΕν.** `/auth` Ήζα ΉηΦΉκ┤
  surface (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider> <source>`) Ά░Α ΈςρΈΣΡ `/login`
  Ήε╝Έκε ΊζκΉΙα. `/login source <provider> <type>` ΉΜιΆ╖ε έΑΦ Ά╕░Ήκ┤ `/auth set` Ήζα
  credential source picker. `routing.py` Ήζα `/auth` CommandSpec, `dispatcher.py`
  Ήζα cmd_auth dispatch, `core/cli/__init__.py` Ήζα TTY_LOCAL_COMMANDS Ήζα
  `/auth` ΈσνΈ▓Ε, `_state.py` Ήζα `COMMAND_MAP` Ήζα `/auth` entry + help line,
  `commands/__init__.py` Ήζα export, `core/cli/commands/auth.py` ΊΝΝΉζ╝ ΉηΡΉ▓┤
  ΈςρΈΣΡ ΉιεΆ▒░. `manage_auth` LLM tool ΉζΑ backwards-compat adapter Έκε ΉειΉπΑ
  έΑΦ Ία╕Ή╢ε ΉΜε `manage_login` Έκε forward (legacy prompts Ία╕Ίβα). Plan vs
  Profile Έ╢ΕΈου Ήζα historical Ά╖╝Ά▒░ (`PlanRegistry` vs `ProfileStore`) ΈΛΦ
  ΉειΉπΑΈΡαΈΡα, ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡ ΉπΕΉηΖΉιΡΉζΑ `/login` ΈΜρΉζ╝ SOT.
- **`/auth` slash command fully removed + `/login source` introduced.**
  The remaining `/auth` surface (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider>
  <source>`) was folded into `/login`. The new `/login source <provider>
  <type>` is the migrated credential-source picker (was `/auth set`,
  PR #1203). Removed: `routing.py` entry, `dispatcher.py` dispatch +
  import, `_TTY_LOCAL_COMMANDS` membership, `_state.py` `COMMAND_MAP` +
  help line, `commands/__init__.py` exports, and the
  `core/cli/commands/auth.py` source file itself. The `manage_auth`
  LLM tool is kept as a backwards-compat adapter that forwards to
  `manage_login` so legacy prompts still work. The underlying Plan vs
  Profile split (`PlanRegistry` vs `ProfileStore`) is unchanged έΑΦ only
  the user-facing entry point is unified.

## [0.98.0] έΑΦ 2026-05-17

### Changed

- **`/login <provider>` έΑΦ provider ΈπΝ parameter Έκε Έ░δΈΛΦ OAuth picker
  + `/auth login` ΉιεΆ▒░.** Ά╕░Ήκ┤ `/login oauth <provider>` Ήζα 2-ΈΜρΉΨ┤ ΊαΧΊΔεΆ░Α
  `/login openai` / `/login anthropic` (alias: `codex`, `chatgpt`,
  `claude`, `claude-code`) Ήζα ΈΜρΉζ╝ ΊΗιΊΒ░ ΉπΕΉηΖΉε╝Έκε ΈΜρΉΙεΊβΦ. provider name
  ΈπΝΉε╝Έκε OAuth flow Ά░Α ΉοΚΉΜε ΈΠβΉηΣ έΑΦ picker surface Ά░Α `/model` Ήζα mirror.
  ΉνΣΈ│╡ ΉπΕΉηΖΉιΡΉζ┤ΈΞα `/auth login` (status display + browser login) Ήζα UI +
  Έ░▒ΉΩΦΈΥε ΈΣΡ helper (`_auth_login_status`, `_sync_oauth_profile_after_login`)
  ΈςρΈΣΡ ΉιεΆ▒░. `/auth` ΈΛΦ profile management ΈπΝ (`add` / `remove` /
  `set <provider> <source>`). Anthropic OAuth path Ά░Α ΉΔΙΈκε `_login_oauth`
  ΉΧΙΉΩΡ Ή╢ΦΆ░ΑΈΡρ έΑΦ local `claude /login` subprocess Ία╕Ή╢ε ΊδΕ macOS keychain
  Ήζα token ΉζΕ `ProfileStore` ΉΩΡ sync. test 41 pass.
- **`/login <provider>` έΑΦ provider-only OAuth picker, `/auth login`
  removed.** The legacy `/login oauth <provider>` two-word form is now
  `/login openai` / `/login anthropic` (aliases: `codex`, `chatgpt`,
  `claude`, `claude-code`) έΑΦ a single provider token runs the OAuth
  flow directly, mirroring the `/model` picker surface. The redundant
  `/auth login` entry point (status display + browser handoff) and
  its `_auth_login_status` / `_sync_oauth_profile_after_login`
  helpers were removed from both UI and backend. `/auth` now hosts
  only profile management (`add` / `remove` / `set <provider>
  <source>`). The Anthropic OAuth path is now folded into
  `_login_oauth` έΑΦ it spawns `claude /login` and then syncs the
  resulting keychain credential into `ProfileStore`. 41 tests pass.

- **`/login <provider>` canonical OAuth entry point.** `/login openai`
  now runs the Codex Plus device-code flow directly, while
  `/login anthropic` delegates to the local Claude Code login flow and
  syncs the resulting keychain credential into `ProfileStore`. The old
  `/login oauth <provider>` spelling is no longer advertised by help,
  onboarding, or tool schema.
- **`/login <provider>`Έξ╝ OAuth ΈΜρΉζ╝ ΉπΕΉηΖΉιΡΉε╝Έκε ΉιΧΈου.** `/login openai`ΈΛΦ
  Codex Plus device-code flowΈξ╝ ΉπΒΉιΣ ΉΜνΊΨΚΊΧαΆ│ι, `/login anthropic`ΉζΑ ΈκεΉ╗υ
  Claude Code login flowΉΩΡ ΉεΕΉηΕΊΧε ΈΤν keychain credentialΉζΕ `ProfileStore`
  Έκε ΈΠβΆ╕░ΊβΦΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν. Ά╕░Ήκ┤ `/login oauth <provider>` ΊαΧΊΔεΈΛΦ help, onboarding,
  tool schemaΉΩΡΉΕε ΈΞΦ Ήζ┤ΉΔΒ ΈΖ╕Ή╢εΊΧαΉπΑ ΉΧΛΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν.

### Removed

- **Legacy `/auth login` UI/backend path.** `/auth` now remains only as
  profile management (`add`, `remove`, `set`); OAuth setup lives under
  `/login <provider>`. The legacy auth-login status/sync helpers were
  removed from the command package export surface.
- **ΈιΙΆ▒░ΉΜε `/auth login` UI/backend Ά▓╜Έκε ΉιεΆ▒░.** `/auth`ΈΛΦ profile Ά┤ΑΈου
  (`add`, `remove`, `set`)ΈπΝ ΈΜ┤ΈΜ╣ΊΧαΆ│ι OAuth ΉΕνΉιΧΉζΑ `/login <provider>`Ά░Α
  ΈΜ┤ΈΜ╣ΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν. Ά╕░Ήκ┤ auth-login status/sync helperΈΠΕ command package export
  surfaceΉΩΡΉΕε ΉιεΆ▒░ΊΨΙΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν.

## [0.97.0] έΑΦ 2026-05-17

### Added

- **`/auth set <provider> <source>` έΑΦ credential source picker (settings
  abstraction).** ΉΔΙ settings ΊΓν `anthropic_credential_source` /
  `openai_credential_source` Ά░Α `auto` / `oauth` / `api_key` / `none`
  ΉνΣ ΊΧαΈΓαΈξ╝ Έ│┤Ήει. `plugins/petri_audit/models.py::to_inspect_model` Ήζ┤
  Έ│╕ Ά░ΤΉζΕ Ήζ╜ΉΨ┤ `claude-*` έΗΤ `anthropic/` ΈαΡΈΛΦ `claude-code/` (Ά╡υΈΠΖ
  OAuth) ΉΓυΉζ┤, `gpt-5.*` έΗΤ `openai/` ΈαΡΈΛΦ `openai-codex/` ΉΓυΉζ┤ prefix
  Έξ╝ ΉηΡΈΠβ ΈπνΊΧΣ. `--use-oauth` Ά░βΉζΑ explicit CLI flag ΈΛΦ settings Έ│┤ΈΜν
  Ήγ░ΉΕι. `/auth` slash command Ά░Α `/auth set ...` subcommand Ή╢ΦΆ░Α
  (Ά╕░Ήκ┤ `login` / `add` / `remove` ΉβΑ Ά│╡Ήκ┤). `/auth login` Ήζα status
  ΊΣεΉΜε ΈΠΕ `get_claude_oauth_metadata` / `get_codex_oauth_metadata` Ήζα
  live keychain ┬╖ JWT payload Έξ╝ surface έΑΦ subscription plan Ήζα Ήζ┤ΈοΕΉζΑ
  Ή╜ΦΈΥεΈ▓ιΉζ┤ΉΛνΉΩΡ hardcode ΉΩΗΉζ┤ credential blob ΉΩΡΉΕε verbatim. picker UI
  (interactive arrow-key, `/model` mirror) ΈΛΦ follow-up PR.
- **`plugins/petri_audit/codex_provider.get_codex_oauth_metadata`.** ΉΜιΆ╖ε
  ΊΩυΊΞ╝ έΑΦ `~/.codex/auth.json` Ήζα JWT payload Ήζα `chatgpt_plan_type` /
  `chatgpt_account_id` / `exp` Έξ╝ dict Ήε╝Έκε Έ░αΊβα. `/auth` picker Ήζα
  OpenAI Ή╕κ label source.

### Changed

- **Anthropic OAuth (Claude subscription) ΉιΧΉ▒Ζ retract.** `core/cli/
  commands/auth.py` Ήζα `/auth login` Ήζα "Anthropic έΑΦ OAuth disabled
  (ToS violation since 2026-01-09)" Έυ╕Ά╡υ + `_sync_oauth_profile_
  after_login` Ήζα `claude` early return ΉιεΆ▒░. `claude_code_provider`
  Ήζα module docstring Ήζα ToS gray-area notice (PR #1202) Έξ╝ ΉιΧΉ▒ΖΉζα
  ΉΔΙ SOT Έκε Ή▒ΕΊΔζ. Claude subscription OAuth Ά░Α Petri audit Ήζα
  auditor / judge / target ΈςρΈΥι role Ήζα cost-zero path Έκε ΈΜνΉΜε
  ΊβεΉΕ▒ΊβΦ. Έ│╕ path ΈΛΦ Anthropic Ήζα documented public OAuth client
  surface Ά░Α ΉΧΕΈΜΙΈψΑΈκε `_warn_policy_once` Ά░Α Ή▓αΉζΝ ΊβεΉΕ▒ ΉΜε WARNING
  ΈκεΆ╖╕Έξ╝ emit (Consumer ToS ┬π3 Ήζα narrow reading Ήζα spirit-area
  risk ΈςΖΉΜε). production / Ήβ╕Έ╢Α Ά│╡Ά░ε ΉΜε `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` Ήζα stock
  `anthropic/` Ά▓╜Έκε Ά╢ΝΉηξ.

### Changed

- **`claude-code` provider: subprocess CLI έΗΤ Anthropic API direct via
  OAuth subscription token.** `plugins/petri_audit/claude_code_provider`
  Ήζα `ClaudeCodeJudgeAPI` (subprocess judge-only, ~400 LOC) Ά░Α
  `ClaudeOAuthAPI` (stock `AnthropicAPI` subclass, ~80 LOC) Έκε Ά╡ΡΉ▓┤.
  macOS keychain entry `Claude Code-credentials` Ήζα OAuth access token
  ΉζΕ Ή╢ΦΉ╢εΊΧ┤ `api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` Ήζα `x-api-key` ΊΩνΈΞΦΈκε
  ΉΓυΉγσ έΑΦ auditor / judge / target 3 role ΈςρΈΣΡ ΉηΡΈΠβ ΉπΑΉδΡ
  (multi-turn + native tool calling). Ά╕░Ήκ┤ judge-only ΉιεΉΧ╜ ΊΧ┤ΉΗΝ. ΉΜιΆ╖ε
  ΊΩυΊΞ╝ `resolve_claude_oauth_token` / `get_claude_oauth_metadata` /
  `is_claude_oauth_available` Ά░Α picker UI (ΊδΕΉΗΞ PR B `/auth`) Ήζα
  source detection ΉΩΡ ΉΓυΉγσΈΡρ. Ά╡υΈΠΖ plan / rate-limit tier ΈΛΦ keychain
  blob ΉΩΡΉΕε verbatim Ή╢ΦΉ╢ε έΑΦ Ή╜ΦΈΥεΈ▓ιΉζ┤ΉΛνΉΩΡ plan enumeration hardcode
  ΉΩΗΉζΝ. ToS spirit Ά▓╜Ά│ι (Consumer ToS ┬π3 Ήζα narrow reading) Έξ╝ Ή▓τ
  ΊβεΉΕ▒ ΉΜε WARNING ΈκεΆ╖╕.
- **`claude-code` provider: subprocess CLI έΗΤ Anthropic API direct via
  OAuth subscription token.** Replaced the subprocess-based judge-only
  adapter with a stock `AnthropicAPI` subclass that resolves the OAuth
  access token from the local `claude` CLI's macOS keychain entry and
  routes calls through `api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`. All Petri
  roles (auditor / judge / target) now work out of the box thanks to
  inspect_ai's native multi-turn + tool-call pipeline. New helpers
  `resolve_claude_oauth_token` / `get_claude_oauth_metadata` /
  `is_claude_oauth_available` expose the keychain state so the
  upcoming `/auth` picker can label the OAuth source with the actual
  subscription plan + rate-limit tier instead of a hardcoded string.
  A one-time WARNING log notes that this path is not part of
  Anthropic's documented public OAuth client surface (Consumer ToS ┬π3
  spirit).

## [0.96.0] έΑΦ 2026-05-16

### Added

- **CLI thinking collapse + Ctrl+O toggle.** CLI reasoning-summary lines now
  collapse at `thinking_end` into a single muted `έεο Thought for έΑο ┬╖ N items`
  header, with the full reasoning history buffered for expansion. During an
  active prompt execution, `Ctrl+O` toggles live thinking between expanded
  streaming lines and a compact still-running header; non-TTY output keeps the
  previous line-by-line behavior.
- **CLI thinking collapse + Ctrl+O toggle.** CLI reasoning summary Έζ╝Ήζ╕Ήζ┤
  `thinking_end` ΉΩΡΉΕε ΈΜρΉζ╝ muted `έεο Thought for έΑο ┬╖ N items` header Έκε
  ΉιΣΊηΙΆ│ι, ΉιΕΉ▓┤ reasoning history ΈΛΦ ΈΜνΉΜε ΊΟ╝Ή╣ι ΉΙα ΉηΙΈΠΕΈκζ ΈΓ┤Έ╢Α buffer ΉΩΡ
  Έ│┤Ά┤ΑΈΡσΈΜΙΈΜν. Prompt ΉΜνΊΨΚ ΉνΣΉΩΡΈΛΦ `Ctrl+O` Έκε live thinking ΉζΕ streaming
  line view ΉβΑ compact still-running header ΉΓυΉζ┤ΉΩΡΉΕε ΉιΕΊβαΊΧι ΉΙα ΉηΙΉε╝Έσ░,
  non-TTY Ή╢εΈιξΉζΑ Ά╕░Ήκ┤ line-by-line ΈΠβΉηΣΉζΕ ΉειΉπΑΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν.

## [0.95.5] έΑΦ 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX digit-base superscripts + grouped scripts.** delimiter-less
  ΉΙαΉΜζ detector Ά░Α `10^2`, `10^-3`, `10^(R_j - R_i)` Ή▓αΈθ╝ ΉΙτΉηΡ base Έξ╝
  Ά░ΑΉπΕ superscript ΊΣεΊαΕΉζΕ inline math Έκε ΉΛ╣Ά▓σΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν. `^(...)` /
  `^{...}` ΈΓ┤Έ╢ΑΉζα nested `_j` ΈΛΦ Έ░ΦΆ╣ξ superscript Έ░σΊΨξΉζΕ ΈΦ░Έζ╝ `╩▓` Έκε
  Έ│ΑΊβαΈΡαΉΨ┤ `10έΒ╜ά┤┐╩▓έΒ╗ά┤┐έΒ▒έΒ╛` / `10ά┤┐╩▓έΒ╗ά┤┐έΒ▒` Έκε Έ│┤Ήζ┤Έσ░, braced superscript Ήζα
  Έ│╡ΊΧσ payload ΉΩΡ bracket fallback Ήζ┤ ΉηαΈς╗ ΉιΒΉγσΈΡαΉΨ┤ `10[...]` Έκε Ά╣ρΉπΑΈΛΦ
  ΊγΝΆ╖ΑΈξ╝ ΈπΚΉΧαΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν. `1_000`, `snake_case`, path false positive ΈΛΦ Ά│ΕΉΗΞ
  text Έκε ΈΓρΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν.
- **CLI LaTeX digit-base superscripts + grouped scripts.** The
  delimiter-less math detector now promotes digit-base superscripts such
  as `10^2`, `10^-3`, and `10^(R_j - R_i)` to inline math. Nested `_j`
  markers inside `^(...)` / `^{...}` inherit the outer superscript
  direction, rendering as `10έΒ╜ά┤┐╩▓έΒ╗ά┤┐έΒ▒έΒ╛` / `10ά┤┐╩▓έΒ╗ά┤┐έΒ▒`, and complex braced
  superscripts no longer hit the broken `10[...]` bracket fallback.
  False positives such as `1_000`, `snake_case`, and paths stay as text.

## [0.95.4] έΑΦ 2026-05-16

### Added

- **autoresearch cross-axis regression gate.** `compute_fitness` Ά░Α
  ΉΔΙ Ήζ╕ΉηΡ `baseline: FitnessBaseline | None = None` ΉζΕ Έ░δΉΧΕ multi-axis
  monotone Ά▓ΑΉΓυΈξ╝ ΉΙαΊΨΚΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν. critical axis (predictive, robustness) Ά░Α
  `baseline - stderr - margin` ΉΧΕΈηαΈκε ΈΨρΉΨ┤ΉπΑΈσ┤ fitness=0.0 Ήε╝Έκε strict
  reject; auxiliary axis (logic, diversity, stability) Ήζα ΊγΝΆ╖ΑΈΛΦ
  `╬╗ ├Ω delta┬▓` (default ╬╗=0.5) squared penalty Έκε weighted sum ΉΩΡΉΕε
  Ή░ρΆ░Ρ. `state/baseline.json` Ήε╝Έκε ΉπΒΉιΕ promote audit Ήζα axes /
  axes_stderr Έξ╝ Έ│┤Ά┤ΑΊΧαΆ│ι `train.py` ΉΜεΉηΣ ΉΜε ΉηΡΈΠβ ΈκεΈΥε. `--no-baseline`
  flag Έκε gate ΈςΖΉΜε Έ╣ΕΊβεΉΕ▒ Ά░ΑΈΛξ. Ά╕░Ήκ┤ single-axis fitness aggregate Ά░Α
  axis Ά░Ε trade-off Έξ╝ Ά░ΡΉ╢░ safety axis Ήζα ΊγΝΆ╖ΑΈξ╝ calibration Ά░εΉΕιΆ│╝
  Ά╡ΡΊβαΊΧαΈΞα Goodhart Ά▓╜ΈκεΈξ╝ Ή░ρΈΜρ.
- **autoresearch cross-axis regression gate.** `compute_fitness` accepts
  a new `baseline: FitnessBaseline | None = None` argument that enforces
  per-axis monotone progress. Critical axes (predictive, robustness)
  trigger a strict reject (fitness = 0.0) when the new score falls below
  `baseline - stderr - margin`; auxiliary axes (logic, diversity,
  stability) absorb regressions as a squared penalty
  (`╬╗ ├Ω delta┬▓`, default ╬╗=0.5). `state/baseline.json` carries the
  parent promote's `axes` + `axes_stderr` between runs and `train.py`
  loads it automatically (use `--no-baseline` to skip). Closes a
  Goodhart path where the previous single-scalar weighted sum could
  promote a hypothesis that traded safety for marginal calibration.
- **autoresearch results.tsv 9-col schema + per-axis stdout.** TSV
  schema Ά░Α `commit / fitness / hallucination_mean / status /
  description` 5 col έΗΤ `commit / fitness / predictive / robustness /
  logic / diversity / stability / verdict / description` 9 col Έκε ΊβΧΉηξ.
  `train.py` ΈΠΕ stdout ΉΩΡ `^<axis>_score:` Έζ╝Ήζ╕ 5 Ά░εΈξ╝ Ή╢ΦΆ░Α emit έΑΦ
  agent Ά░Α `grep "^[a-z]*_score:"` ΊΧε Έ▓ΙΉε╝Έκε results.tsv Ήζα axis
  column 5 Ά░εΈξ╝ Ή▒ΕΉγ╕ ΉΙα ΉηΙΉζΝ.
- **autoresearch results.tsv 9-column schema + per-axis stdout.** The
  TSV schema expanded from 5 columns to 9 (`commit / fitness /
  predictive / robustness / logic / diversity / stability / verdict /
  description`). `train.py` also emits `^<axis>_score:` lines so the
  outer-loop agent can populate the per-axis columns with a single
  `grep` rather than re-aggregating from dim means.
- **autoresearch closed-loop fitness extraction.** `geode audit` Ήζ┤ archive
  ΈΡε `.eval` ΉΩΡΉΕε per-dim mean + stderr Έξ╝ ΉπΣΆ│ΕΊΧ┤ stdout ΈπΙΉπΑΈπΚΉΩΡ ΊΧε ΉνΕ
  JSON Ήε╝Έκε emit ΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν (`{"dim_means": ..., "dim_stderr": ...}`). ΉΔΙ ΈςρΈΥΙ
  `core.audit.dim_extractor` Ά░Α `inspect_ai.log.read_eval_log` Έκε sample
  scores Έξ╝ Ήζ╜Ά│ι ddof=1 stderr Έξ╝ Ά│ΕΉΓ░. `autoresearch/train.py::run_audit`
  ΉζΑ 4-tuple `(dim_means, dim_stderr, audit_seconds, total_seconds)` Έξ╝
  Έ░αΊβαΊΧαΈΠΕΈκζ ΊβΧΉηξ έΑΦ outer loop Ά░Α fitness ΈπΝ grep ΊΧαΈΛΦ Karpathy ΊΝρΊΕ┤ ΉειΉπΑ.
- **autoresearch closed-loop fitness extraction.** `geode audit` now
  emits a final JSON line `{"dim_means": ..., "dim_stderr": ...}`
  derived from the archived `.eval` so `autoresearch/train.py` can grep
  it without re-reading inspect_ai's log format. A new module
  `core.audit.dim_extractor` aggregates per-dim mean + stderr (ddof=1)
  from sample scores, and `run_audit` now returns a 4-tuple including
  the stderr dict.

### Changed

- **autoresearch stability axis derives from stderr.** 5-axis fitness Ήζα
  stability ΊΧφΉζ┤ placeholder 0.5 ΈΝΑΉΜι `1 / (1 + mean_stderr)` Έκε Ά│ΕΉΓ░ΈΡσΈΜΙΈΜν
  (ΉΜνΉιε audit Ήζα ``dim_stderr`` Ά░Α Έ╣ΕΉΨ┤ΉηΙΉζΕ ΈΧΝΈπΝ placeholder Έκε fallback).
  bounded (0, 1] + monotone-decreasing ΊΧε Ά░Τ έΑΦ ΈΜρΉζ╝ axis Ά░Α fitness Έξ╝
  3.13├Ω Ά╣ΝΉπΑ ΈΒΝΉΨ┤ΉαυΈι╕ΈΞα old `1 / stderr_mean` ΉΜζΉζα Goodhart ΉεΕΊΩαΉζΕ Ή░ρΈΜρ.
  dry-run baseline ΉζΑ placeholder Ά▓╜ΈκεΈξ╝ Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε ΉειΉπΑ (`fitness=0.535895`
  Έ│ΑΈΠβ ΉΩΗΉζΝ).
- **autoresearch stability axis derived from stderr.** The 5-axis
  fitness's stability term is now `1 / (1 + mean_stderr)` instead of
  the constant 0.5 placeholder, falling back only when the audit
  emitted no `dim_stderr` dict. Bounded in (0, 1] and monotone-
  decreasing έΑΦ the previous `1 / stderr_mean` formula was unbounded
  and could swing one axis to 3.13├Ω of all others, a Goodhart risk.
  The dry-run baseline still uses the placeholder, so the
  `fitness=0.535895` plumbing contract is unchanged.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX slash-division detector + uppercase subscript display.**
  delimiter-less ΉΙαΉΜζ detector Ά░Α `/` ΊΧαΈΓαΈπΝ Έ│┤Ά│ι path Έκε ΉανΊΝΡΊΧαΈΞα Έυ╕ΉιεΈξ╝
  ΉΙαΉιΧΊΨΙΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν. `E_i = 1/1 + 10^(R_j - R_i)/400` Ήζα ΈπΙΉπΑΈπΚ `R_i` ΈΛΦ
  Ήζ┤Ήιε `Rά╡λ` inline math Έκε ΉηκΊηΙΆ│ι, `foo/bar/baz.py` / `src/main.tsx`
  Ά░βΉζΑ ΉΜνΉιε path ΈΛΦ Ά│ΕΉΗΞ text Έκε ΈΓρΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν. Unicode ΉΧΕΈηαΉ▓ρΉηΡΉΩΡ ΉΩΗΈΛΦ
  ΈΝΑΈυ╕ΉηΡ Latin payload ΈΛΦ raw `_` ΈΝΑΉΜι bracket fallback (`╧Ε_P` έΗΤ `╧Ε[P]`)
  Ήε╝Έκε ΊΣεΉΜεΊΧ┤ ΊΕ░Έψ╕ΈΕΡΉΩΡΉΕε marker ΈΙΕΉΙαΈξ╝ ΊΦ╝ΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν.
- **CLI LaTeX slash-division detector + uppercase subscript display.**
  The delimiter-less math detector no longer treats any nearby `/` as
  path evidence. `E_i = 1/1 + 10^(R_j - R_i)/400` now captures the final
  `R_i` as `Rά╡λ`, while real paths such as `foo/bar/baz.py` and
  `src/main.tsx` remain plain text. Unsupported uppercase Latin subscript
  payloads now use a bracket fallback (`╧Ε_P` έΗΤ `╧Ε[P]`) instead of leaking
  the raw `_` marker.

## [0.95.3] έΑΦ 2026-05-16

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX bare script Unicode rendering.** Tier 1 LaTeX ΈιΝΈΞΦΈθυΆ░Α
  `pylatexenc` Ή╢εΈιξ Ήζ┤ΊδΕ `_i`, `_1`, `^2` Ά░βΉζΑ delimiter-less
  subscript/superscript ΊΗιΊΒ░ΉζΕ Unicode ΉΧΕΈηα/ΉεΕΉ▓ρΉηΡΈκε ΊδΕΉ▓αΈουΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν.
  ΉπΑΉδΡ Έυ╕ΉηΡΆ░Α ΉΩΗΈΛΦ ΊΗιΊΒ░ΉζΑ ΉδΡΈυ╕ marker Έξ╝ Έ│┤Ήκ┤ΊΧ┤ `h_έΙη` Ά░βΉζΑ ΊΣεΆ╕░Έξ╝
  Έ╢ΑΈ╢Ε Έ│ΑΊβαΊΧαΉπΑ ΉΧΛΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν.
- **CLI LaTeX bare script Unicode rendering.** Tier 1 now post-processes
  `pylatexenc` output so delimiter-less scripts such as `h_i`, `w_1`, and
  `x^2` render as Unicode glyphs. Tokens containing unsupported script
  characters remain raw atomically, preserving forms like `h_έΙη` instead of
  producing mixed output.

## [0.95.2] έΑΦ 2026-05-16

### Added

- **CLI system prompt math-formatting instruction.** GEODE Ήζα Ά╕░Έ│╕ LLM
  prompt Ά░Α ΉΙαΉΜζ Ή╢εΈιξ Ά╖εΉ╣βΉζΕ ΈςΖΉΜεΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν: inline ΉΙαΉΜζΉζΑ `$...$`, display
  ΉΙαΉΜζΉζΑ ΈΠΖΈο╜ ΉνΕΉζα `$$...$$` Έκε Ά░ΡΉΜ╕ΈΠΕΈκζ ΉππΉζΑ ΉαΙΉΜεΈξ╝ ΊΠυΊΧρΊΨΙΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν. Ήζ┤
  ΉπΑΉΜεΈΛΦ `PromptAssembler` Ά▓╜ΈκεΉβΑ interactive CLI Ήζα `AgenticLoop`
  system prompt Ά▓╜ΈκεΉΩΡ ΈςρΈΣΡ ΉιΒΉγσΈΡσΈΜΙΈΜν.
- **CLI system prompt math-formatting instruction.** The default LLM prompt
  now tells the model to wrap inline math in `$...$` and display math in
  standalone `$$...$$` blocks, with compact examples. The rule is wired into
  both `PromptAssembler` and the interactive CLI `AgenticLoop` system prompt.

- **CLI LaTeX Tier 3 (graphics inline) έΑΦ capability detection scaffold.**
  CLI LaTeX Ήζα frontier 5-tier Ήκ░ΉΓυ Ά▓░Ά│╝ LLM CLI 6 ΈΠΕΆ╡υ (Claude Code /
  Codex CLI / Aider / glow / mdcat / bat) ΈςρΈΣΡ Tier 0 (raw), GEODE ΈπΝ
  Tier 1+2 cascade. Tier 3 (image inline via Kitty / SIXEL graphics
  protocols) Ή╢ΦΆ░Α ΉΜε **ΉειΉζ╝ΊΧε 4-tier ΊΗ╡ΊΧσ CLI agent**. Έ│╕ PR ΉζΑ scaffold:
  - `core/ui/latex_graphics.py` έΑΦ `detect_graphics_capability()` Ά░Α
    `TERM=xterm-kitty` / `TERM=wezterm-*` / `TERM=xterm-ghostty` /
    `KITTY_WINDOW_ID` / `WEZTERM_PANE` / `WEZTERM_EXECUTABLE` /
    `GHOSTTY_RESOURCES_DIR` / `KONSOLE_VERSION` (Kitty graphics protocol
    family) + `mlterm` / `foot` (SIXEL) conservative allow-list + non-TTY
    ΊγΝΊΦ╝ + `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS_FORCE` / `_DISABLE` operator override.
    `render_latex_image()` ΈΛΦ public API ΉΜεΆ╖╕ΈΕΙΉ▓α pin, ΊαΕΉηυ
    `NotImplementedError` (ΈΜνΉζΝ PR ΉΩΡΉΕε matplotlib ΈαΡΈΛΦ sympy.preview
    + dvipng έΗΤ PNG έΗΤ Kitty/SIXEL escape wire).
  - `graphics_opt_in_active()` έΑΦ env `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS` truthy
    Ή▓┤ΊΒυ. capability detect ΉβΑ Έ╢ΕΈουΈΡαΉΨ┤ matplotlib import Έ╣ΕΉγσΉζΕ
    opt-out ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡΆ░Α ΉΧΙ ΉπΛΉΨ┤ΉπΑΆ▓Ν.
  - 18 ΉΜιΆ╖ε test (`tests/test_latex_graphics.py`): unknown / Kitty
    family 5 / SIXEL 2 / force-disable / force-protocol / invalid
    force / non-TTY / opt-in truthy/falsy / scaffold NotImplementedError
    + ΉζαΈΠΕΈΡε ΈσΦΉΜεΉπΑ.
  - Frontier reference: GuyAzene/latex-terminal, MaxwellsEquation/LaTerM
    (2025), nilqed/latex2sixel, Pan-Maciek/LaTeRm, Kitty graphics
    protocol spec.
- **CLI LaTeX Tier 3 (graphics inline) έΑΦ capability detection
  scaffold.** Adds `core/ui/latex_graphics.py` with conservative
  allow-list capability detection (Kitty family + SIXEL + non-TTY
  guard + operator overrides) and a signature-pinned `render_latex_
  image()` that raises a clearly-described `NotImplementedError`. The
  follow-up PR will wire matplotlib (or sympy.preview + dvipng) έΗΤ PNG
  έΗΤ Kitty / SIXEL escape sequences. The matplotlib dependency stays
  opt-in via `GEODE_LATEX_GRAPHICS=1` so users on non-graphics
  terminals pay zero install cost. 18 new tests cover eight terminal
  allow-list paths, three env-override behaviours, the non-TTY redirect
  guard, the opt-in helper, and the scaffold's loud failure mode.

### Changed

- **Phase 1b έΑΦ Long-term Recall: JSON 20-trim ΊΧ┤Ήιε + DB SoT ΉιΕΊβα + layout
  v4 migration.** Hermes ΊζκΉΙα plan (`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-
  strengths-absorption.md`) Ήζα 1b. PR #1151 Ήζα dual-write (JSON SoT, DB
  mirror) Έξ╝ ΈΤνΉπΣΉΨ┤ SQLite `messages` ΊΖΝΉζ┤Έ╕ΦΉζ┤ SoT, JSON ΉζΑ hot cache.
  - `core/runtime_state/session_checkpoint.py` Ήζα
    `CHECKPOINT_MAX_MESSAGES` Έξ╝ 20έΗΤ0 (no trim). `save()` Ά░Α DB Έρ╝ΉιΑ
    write ΊδΕ JSON hot cache (full list, no trim) write. `load()` Ά░Α
    DB Ήγ░ΉΕι (`_load_messages_from_db`), DB Ά░Α Έ╣ΕΉΨ┤ΉηΙΉζΕ ΈΧΝΈπΝ JSON
    fallback έΑΦ pre-PR-1151 / dual-write race loser Ία╕Ίβα.
  - `core/wiring/layout_migrator.py` Ήζα `GEODE_LAYOUT_VERSION` 3έΗΤ4 +
    ΉΜιΆ╖ε `_migrate_v3_to_v4()` έΑΦ `~/.geode/projects/*/sessions/*/
    messages.json` Ήζ╝Ά┤Ε backfill. ΉΗΡΉΔΒ ΊΝΝΉζ╝ skip + WARN, idempotent
    (UNIQUE(session_id, seq)), ΉπΕΊΨΚΈξι INFO every 10 sessions, fresh
    install graceful skip.
  - `tools.json` ΉζΑ backward compat Ήε╝Έκε hot cache ΉειΉπΑ. ΉΜιΆ╖ε 7 test
    + Ά╕░Ήκ┤ `test_message_trimming` ΉζΕ `test_no_trim_full_history_
    preserved` Έκε ΉζαΈψ╕ ΉιΕΊβα.
- **Phase 1b έΑΦ Long-term Recall: JSON trim removed, SoT flipped to
  SQLite, layout v4 migration.** Inverts PR #1151's dual-write
  contract έΑΦ the SQLite `messages` table is now the source of truth
  and `messages.json` is a full-list hot cache for offline tooling.
  `CHECKPOINT_MAX_MESSAGES` zeroed (20έΗΤ0, "no trim"); `save()` writes
  the DB first then the untrimmed JSON; `load()` reads DB-first with a
  JSON fallback for legacy sessions. `GEODE_LAYOUT_VERSION` bumped
  3έΗΤ4 with `_migrate_v3_to_v4()` doing an idempotent corrupt-tolerant
  backfill of every pre-existing `messages.json` into the per-project
  `sessions.db`. Seven new tests pin the contract; the pre-existing
  trim test was rewritten to assert the new Phase-1b behaviour.

### Documentation

- **Autoresearch gen 0 baseline ΉΜεΈΠΕ έΑΦ Anthropic credit Ή░ρΈΜρΉε╝Έκε BLOCKED.**
  PR #1159 Ήζα wrapper-override hook + PR #1165/#1169/#1171 Ήζα LaTeX
  rendering fix Ήζ┤ΊδΕ Ή▓τ real-mode audit Ία╕Ή╢ε ΉΜεΈΠΕ. 3 ΈΜρΆ│Ε fail-and-fix:
  (1) `inspect` CLI Έψ╕ΉΕνΉ╣α έΗΤ `uv sync --extra audit`. (2) Anthropic
  Ήζ╕Ήοζ ΊΩνΈΞΦ Έψ╕ΉιΕΈΜυ έΗΤ `~/.geode/.env` Ήζα key Έξ╝ `env` prefix Έκε inspect
  subprocess Ά╣ΝΉπΑ propagate. (3) Anthropic API credit balance Έ╢ΑΉκ▒ έΑΦ
  Ήβ╕Έ╢Α Ή░ρΈΜρ ΉΓυΉει. Surrogate baseline Ήε╝Έκε 2026-05-15 Ήζα cross-model paired
  ╬Φ (`docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-insights.md`) Ά░Α gen 1 ablation Ήζα
  starting point Έκε valid. `docs/audits/2026-05-16-autoresearch-gen0-
  baseline.md` ΉΩΡ ΉΜεΈΠΕ ΊΛ╕ΈιΙΉζ┤ΉΛν + surrogate + ΈΜνΉζΝ ΉΜεΈΠΕ Ήα╡ΉΖα 3 ΉλΖ ΉιΧΈου.
  Ή╢ΦΉ▓ε: `--auditor claude-code/sonnet` (Claude Max OAuth, $0 PAYG).
- **Autoresearch gen 0 baseline attempt έΑΦ BLOCKED by Anthropic credit.**
  First real-mode audit invocation after PRs #1159/#1165/#1169/#1171.
  Three sequential fail-and-fix steps (`inspect` CLI install, env-var
  propagation to the inspect subprocess, then a hard wall on Anthropic
  credit). The yesterday cross-model paired-╬Φ surrogate at
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-insights.md` remains a valid starting
  point for the gen-1 nine-hypothesis ablation. The next-attempt note
  recommends `--auditor claude-code/sonnet` for zero PAYG cost via the
  Claude Max subscription quota, contingent on PR #1147's adapter
  supporting the auditor role.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX ΈιΝΈΞΦΈπΒ έΑΦ bare subscript/superscript + Unicode math ΈΙΕΉ╢ε.**
  delimiter ΉΩΗΈΛΦ fallback Ήζ┤ Ά╕░Ήκ┤ΉΩΡΈΛΦ `P_{t-1}` Ά░βΉζΑ braced script ΉβΑ
  allow-list macro ΈπΝ ΉηκΉΧΕ `y^╬ΦT_t,n`, `S^(i)_t,n`, `X_t-9:t,n,:`,
  `έΙγx` Ά░βΉζΑ LLM Ή╢εΈιξΉζ┤ raw Έκε ΈΓρΉΧαΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν. `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` Έξ╝
  math-shaped line context + index-like bare script Έκε ΊβΧΉηξΊΧαΆ│ι, `έΙγ` /
  Greek / comparison / arrow ΈΥ▒ Unicode math glyph token ΉζΕ inline math
  segment Έκε ΉΛ╣Ά▓σΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν. Markdown inline/fenced code, `snake_case`,
  slash paths, `**bold**`, `*x*` ΈΛΦ Ά│ΕΉΗΞ text Έκε ΉειΉπΑΈΡσΈΜΙΈΜν.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering έΑΦ bare subscript/superscript + Unicode math leaks.**
  The delimiter-less fallback now catches math-shaped bare scripts and
  Unicode math glyph tokens such as `y^╬ΦT_t,n`, `S^(i)_t,n`, `X_t-9:t,n,:`,
  and `έΙγx`. The wider detector is guarded by code-span/path/snake-case/
  Markdown-emphasis skips so ordinary prose and code remain untouched.
- **CLI prompt CJK ΉηΖΈιξ redraw lag.** prompt_toolkit thin-CLI ΉηΖΈιξΉΩΡΉΕε
  ΊΧεΆ╕Α Ά░βΉζΑ wide character Έξ╝ ΊΔΑΉζ┤ΊΧΣΊΧι ΈΧΝ ΉπΒΉιΕ Ά╕ΑΉηΡΆ░Α ΈΜνΉζΝ keystroke ΉιΕΆ╣ΝΉπΑ
  ΊβΦΈσ┤ΉΩΡ ΈΓαΊΔΑΈΓαΉπΑ ΉΧΛΈΛΦ ghost ΊαΕΉΔΒΉζΕ ΉΙαΉιΧΊΨΙΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν. `<any>` printable
  input binding Ήζ┤ `event.data` Έξ╝ ΉιΧΉΔΒ `insert_text()` Ά▓╜ΈκεΈκε ΈΕμΉζΑ ΈΤν
  `event.app.invalidate()` Έξ╝ Ία╕Ή╢εΊΧ┤ ΉΓ╜ΉηΖ ΉπΒΊδΕ renderer repaint Έξ╝
  ΉαΙΉΧ╜ΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν. Enter / Escape+Enter / Backspace / Delete Ά░βΉζΑ Ά╕░Ήκ┤
  binding ΉζΑ ΉειΉπΑΈΡαΈσ░, wildcard handler ΈΛΦ Έ╣ΕΉΨ┤ ΉηΙΆ▒░ΈΓα non-printable Ήζ╕
  key data Έξ╝ ΉΓ╜ΉηΖΊΧαΉπΑ ΉΧΛΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν.
- **CLI prompt CJK insertion redraw lag.** Fixes the thin-CLI
  prompt_toolkit prompt where newly typed wide characters such as Korean
  Hangul could stay visually hidden until the next keystroke. A printable
  `<any>` input binding now forwards `event.data` through the normal
  `insert_text()` path, then calls `event.app.invalidate()` so the renderer
  repaints immediately after insertion. Existing Enter, Escape+Enter,
  Backspace, and Delete bindings are preserved, and the wildcard handler
  ignores empty or non-printable key data.
- **CLI streaming Markdown cleanup.** Thin CLI raw `stream` output now tracks
  plain daemon-console spans that look like assistant Markdown and clears that
  transient region at turn stop, before the final `result.text` payload is
  rendered through the existing Markdown + LaTeX renderer. ANSI/Rich stream
  output and structured agentic events continue to render in place.
- **CLI ΉΛνΊΛ╕ΈουΈ░Ξ Markdown ΉιΧΈου.** thin CLI Ά░Α daemon-console Ήζα plain
  `stream` ΉνΣ assistant Markdown Ήε╝Έκε Έ│┤Ήζ┤ΈΛΦ Ά╡υΆ░ΕΉζΕ Ή╢ΦΉιΒΊΧαΆ│ι, turn ΉλΖΈμΝ ΉΜε
  Ή╡εΉλΖ `result.text` Έξ╝ Ά╕░Ήκ┤ Markdown + LaTeX renderer Έκε ΈΜνΉΜε Ά╖╕ΈουΆ╕░ ΉιΕΉΩΡ
  ΊΧ┤ΈΜ╣ ΉηΕΉΜε raw Ά╡υΆ░ΕΉζΕ ΉπΑΉδΒΈΜΙΈΜν. ANSI/Rich stream Ή╢εΈιξΆ│╝ structured
  agentic event ΈιΝΈΞΦΈπΒΉζΑ Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε ΉειΉπΑΈΡσΈΜΙΈΜν.
- **CLI LaTeX ΈιΝΈΞΦΈπΒ έΑΦ delimiter-less ΈπνΊΒυΈκε ΈΙΕΉ╢ε heuristic.** PR
  #1165/#1169 Ήζα wiring Ήζ┤ `\(...\)` / `$...$` / `\[...\]` Ά░βΉζΑ ΈςΖΉΜεΉιΒ
  delimiter Ά░Α ΉηΙΈΛΦ Ά▓╜Ήγ░ΈπΝ cover ΊΧαΉΩυ LLM Ήζ┤ delimiter ΉΩΗΉζ┤ prose ΉΧΙΉΩΡ
  ΈπνΊΒυΈκεΈξ╝ emit ΊΧαΈΛΦ Ά▓╜Ήγ░ (ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡ 2026-05-16 Έ│┤Ά│ι: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1})
  / P_{t-1}` raw ΈΖ╕Ή╢ε) ΊγΝΆ╖Α.
  - `core/ui/latex.py` ΉΩΡ `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex Ή╢ΦΆ░Α έΑΦ ΈΣΡ ΉλΒΉζΑ
    ΊαΧΉΜζΈπΝ catch: (1) **braced subscript/superscript token** (`r_{t-1}`,
    `P_{t+5}`, `x^{2}`, `W_{i,j}^{T}`) έΑΦ `{έΑο}` Ά░Α ΉπΒΉιΣ ΈΦ░Έζ╝ΉΧ╝ ΊΧαΈψΑΈκε
    `snake_case`/`file_name`/`r_t` Ά░βΉζΑ Ήζ╝Έ░α underscore identifier ΈΛΦ
    ΉιΙΈΝΑ ΈπνΉ╣α X. (2) **allow-list ΈπνΊΒυΈκε** (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
    `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`έΑΥ`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty` ΈΥ▒) +
    word boundary `(?![A-Za-z])` έΑΦ `\alphanumeric` Ά░βΉζΑ prefix collision
    ΊγΝΊΦ╝. Ήγ░ΉΕιΉΙεΉεΕΈΛΦ ΈςρΈΥι delimited match Ήζ┤ΊδΕ (ΈπΙΉπΑΈπΚ fallback).
  - 7 ΉΜιΆ╖ε test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`): ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡ Έ│┤Ά│ι case +
    braced sub/sup multi-token + snake_case/path false-positive ΊγΝΊΦ╝ +
    macro allow-list + `\alphanumeric` boundary + braced superscript.
  - ΊΧεΆ│Ε: `r_t` (braces ΉΩΗΈΛΦ ΈΜρΉζ╝ character subscript) ΈΛΦ ΉζαΈΠΕΉιΒ Έ╣ΕΈπνΉ╣α
    έΑΦ Markdown emphasis `_text_` ΉβΑ Ή╢σΈΠΝ ΊγΝΊΦ╝ + Ήζ╝Έ░α Έ│ΑΉΙαΈςΖ false
    positive Ή░ρΈΜρ Ήγ░ΉΕι. LLM Ήζ┤ ΈςΖΉΜεΉιΒ `r_{t}` ΊαΧΉΜζΉζΕ ΉΥ░Ά▒░ΈΓα
    `\(...\)` Ήε╝Έκε wrap ΊΧ┤ΉΧ╝ ΉιΧΊβΧ Έ│ΑΊβα.
  - follow-up verifier Έ│┤Ά░Χ: delimiter-less allow-list ΉΩΡ `\mathbb`,
    `\mathcal`, `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`,
    `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`, Έ╣ΕΆ╡Ρ/ΉπΣΊΧσ/ΈΖ╝Έου/ΊβΦΉΓ┤ΊΣε ΈπνΊΒυΈκεΈξ╝ Ή╢ΦΆ░ΑΊΧαΆ│ι,
    `\dfrac`/`\tfrac` ΈΛΦ Tier 1 ΉΩΡΉΕε `\frac` Ή▓αΈθ╝ `a/b` Έκε ΈιΝΈΞΦΈΡαΈΠΕΈκζ
    ΉιΧΆ╖εΊβΦ.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering έΑΦ delimiter-less macro leak heuristic.** PRs
  #1165/#1169 wired the renderer for explicit delimiters (`\(...\)` /
  `$...$` / `\[...\]`) but LLM responses that emit LaTeX *without*
  delimiters (the user's 2026-05-16 report: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1}) /
  P_{t-1}` showing as raw macros) still leaked. The new
  `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex catches two narrow forms: (1) braced
  subscript/superscript tokens (`r_{t-1}`, `P_{t+5}^{2}`,
  `W_{i,j}^{T}`) έΑΦ the `{έΑο}` requirement keeps `snake_case`, file
  paths, and bare-letter subscripts like `r_t` immune, and (2) an
  allow-list of backslash macros (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
  `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`έΑΥ`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty`, έΑο) with a
  word-boundary guard so `\alphanumeric` is not misread as `\alpha`.
  The heuristic fires after every delimited pattern, so explicit
  `\(έΑο\)` math still takes precedence. Seven new tests in
  `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` pin the user-reported case, false-
  positive immunity for `snake_case` / paths, the macro allow-list,
  the word boundary, and braced superscripts. Known limit: bare-letter
  subscripts like `r_t` stay literal έΑΦ adding them would conflict with
  Markdown's `_text_` emphasis and create false positives across
  ordinary prose; the LLM must use `r_{t}` or wrap in `\(...\)`.
  Follow-up verifier hardening expands the delimiter-less allow-list for
  frequent LLM set / logic / prose math macros (`\mathbb`, `\mathcal`,
  `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`, `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`,
  comparisons, arrows, and quantifiers) and normalizes `\dfrac` /
  `\tfrac` through the Tier 1 `\frac` path so they render as `a/b`
  instead of collapsing to adjacent numerator / denominator text.

- **CLI LaTeX ΈιΝΈΞΦΈπΒ έΑΦ multi-line source Ήζα vertical ΉνΕΆ╕ΜΆ╕░ ΊγΝΆ╖Α.**
  PR #1141/#1165 Ήζα wiring Ήζ┤ΊδΕ LLM Ήζ┤ `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` Ά░βΉζΑ
  ΈπνΊΒυΈκεΈξ╝ multi-line LaTeX source Έκε emit ΊΧαΈσ┤ (`\frac{<newline>num
  <newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`), pylatexenc Ά░Α source line break
  Έξ╝ Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε Έ│┤Ήκ┤ΊΧαΉΩυ ΊΕ░Έψ╕ΈΕΡΉΩΡΉΕε ΈςρΈΥι ΊΗιΊΒ░Ήζ┤ ΊΧε ΉνΕΉΦσ vertical Έκε ΈΛαΉΨ┤ΉκΝΉζΝ
  (ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡ Έ│┤Ά│ι 2026-05-16: IC_t / = / έΙΣ_i=1^N / ( / S_t,i - S╠Ε_t,: / )
  ... 16+ ΉνΕ).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` Ήζ┤ explicit LaTeX row break (`\\`)
    Έξ╝ Έ│┤Ήκ┤ΊΧαΈσ┤ΉΕε rendered line ΈΓ┤Έ╢ΑΉζα whitespace run ΉζΕ single space Έκε
    collapse. LaTeX source line break ΈΛΦ mathematical ΉζαΈψ╕Ά░Α ΉΩΗΉε╝ΈψΑΈκε
    inline + block fallback Ήζα vertical stack ΉζΕ ΈπΚΈΡα, `cases`/`aligned`
    ΉΛνΊΔΑΉζ╝Ήζα ΉζαΈΠΕΉιΒ ΊΨΚ Ά╡υΈ╢ΕΉζΑ ΉειΉπΑ. Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) ΈΛΦ Έυ┤Ά┤Α.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` Ήζα `[^\n]+?` έΗΤ `[\s\S]+?` έΑΦ
    multi-line Έ│╕Έυ╕Ήζα `\(...\)` ΈΠΕ Ήζ╕ΉΜζΊΧαΈΠΕΈκζ. Ήζ┤ΉιΕΉΩΦ inline regex Ά░Α
    ΈπνΉ╣α ΉΜνΊΝρ ΉΜε Έ│╕Έυ╕Ήζ┤ raw ΊΖΞΉΛνΊΛ╕Έκε ΊζαΈθυ `\frac`/`\sum` ΈπνΊΒυΈκεΆ░Α Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε
    ΈΖ╕Ή╢εΈΡΡΉζΝ.
  - 3 ΉΜιΆ╖ε ΊγΝΆ╖Α test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` Ήζα
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline` +
    `_block`, `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) έΑΦ IC_t
    Pearson ΉΔΒΆ┤ΑΆ│ΕΉΙα ΉΜζΉζα 7-line LaTeX source Ά░Α inline (`\(...\)`) /
    block (`\[...\]`) ΈΣΡ ΊαΧΉΜζΉΩΡΉΕε ΈςρΈΣΡ single-paragraph Έκε ΊζΡΈοΕ + raw
    ΈπνΊΒυΈκε leak 0 + math ΊΗιΊΒ░ (έΙΣ, έΙγ) Ή╢εΊαΕ + Ή╢εΈιξ line ΉΙα cap. Ή╢ΦΆ░ΑΈκε
    `cases` Ήζα explicit row break Έ│┤Ήκ┤ΉζΕ pin. pre-fix Ήζα 16+
    vertical-stack regression Ή░ρΈΜρ.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering έΑΦ vertical-stack regression from multi-line
  source.** After PR #1141/#1165 wired the renderer, an LLM emitting
  `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` with source-level line breaks
  (`\frac{<newline>num<newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`) caused
  pylatexenc to preserve every newline verbatim, which a narrow terminal
  printed as a vertical stack of single tokens (`IC_t` / `=` / `έΙΣ_i=1^N`
  / `(` / `S_t,i - S╠Ε_t,:` / `)` / έΑο, 16+ lines).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` now preserves explicit LaTeX row
    breaks (`\\`) while collapsing whitespace runs inside each rendered
    line to a single space. LaTeX source line breaks have no mathematical
    meaning έΑΦ flattening preserves the math while restoring inline flow,
    without erasing intentional `cases`/`aligned` rows. Affects inline
    and block Tier 1 fallback; Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) is untouched.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` widens `[^\n]+?` to `[\s\S]+?` so
    multi-line `\(έΑο\)` segments are recognised. Pre-fix, the inline
    regex silently failed on a multi-line body and the raw `\frac` /
    `\sum` / `\bar` macros leaked through as plain prose.
  - 3 new regression tests (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`,
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline`,
    `_block`, and `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) drive
    a 7-line IC_t Pearson-correlation formula through both `\(έΑο\)` and
    `\[έΑο\]` modes and assert: (a) math symbols (`έΙΣ`, `έΙγ`) reach the
    output, (b) no raw `\`-macros leak, (c) the math block stays within a
    sane line-count cap. The third test pins explicit `cases` row breaks,
    blocking both the pre-fix 16-line regression and over-collapse.

### Infrastructure

- **CLI UI/UX regression tests for LaTeX rendering έΑΦ Stage A/B/C Ή╢ΦΆ░Α.**
  PR #1165 Ήζα `_render_text_with_latex` wiring Ήζ┤ ΊΨξΊδΕ refactor Έκε
  silently ΊγΝΆ╖ΑΊΧαΉπΑ Ές╗ΊΧαΆ▓Ν ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡ Ά░ΑΉΜε ΈΠβΉηΣΉΩΡ anchor ΊΧαΈΛΦ 3-stage ΊγΝΆ╖Α
  Έ│┤Ία╕ ΉΛΙΊΛ╕. `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` 21 ΉΜιΆ╖ε.
  - **Stage A (Component capture, 9 cases)** έΑΦ `Rich.Console(file=StringIO,
    force_terminal=False, theme=GEODE_THEME, color_system=None)` Έκε ΉΜνΉιε
    ΈιΝΈΞΦ Ά▓░Ά│╝Έξ╝ buffer ΉΩΡ capture ΊδΕ plain-text substring Ά▓ΑΉοζ. ΊΝρΊΕ┤:
    pure prose (no math) / `\[...\]` block / `\(...\)` inline / `$x$`
    inline / `$3.00` Ά░ΑΆ▓σ false positive ΊγΝΊΦ╝ / `\begin{equation}` env /
    mixed dollar+bracket / segment ordering. raw delimiter ΉηΦΉηυ 0 ΊβΧΉζ╕.
  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrize)** έΑΦ
    `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` / `\lim` / `\int` Ά░ΒΆ░ΒΉΩΡ ΈΝΑΊΧ┤ SymPy
    `pretty()` Ή╢εΈιξΉζα **structural** ΉΗΞΉΕ▒ΈπΝ Ά▓ΑΉοζ (substring group ΉνΣ
    ΊΧαΈΓα + Ή╡εΉΗΝ line count). SymPy upgrade ΉΜε fraction-bar Ήζα `έΦΑ` έΗΦ `-`
    Ά░βΉζΑ cosmetic shift Έυ┤Ά┤Α. brittleness 0.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 test)** έΑΦ `_render_ipc_response`
    Έξ╝ hand-crafted IPC dict Έκε ΉπΒΉιΣ Ία╕Ή╢ε. result + bracket math /
    pure markdown fallback / error / streamed=True Ήζα tool Έψ╕ΉνΣΈ│╡ /
    streamed=False Ήζα fallback summary / 4 lifecycle ack ΈΥνΉζ┤ silent
    drop. serveέΗΤthin-CLI Ήζα ΉιΕΉ▓┤ print path cover.
  - Spinner thread leak ΊγΝΊΦ╝ (PR #1165 follow-up Ήζα lesson): ΈςρΈΥι
    test Ά░Α `force_terminal=False` non-TTY console ΉΓυΉγσ, ΈςΖΉΜεΉιΒ
    `EventRenderer.start_activity()` Ία╕Ή╢ε 0. ΈΜνΈξ╕ test Ήζα
    `@patch("...time.sleep")` ΉΩΡ `time.sleep(0.08)` ΈΙΕΉιΒ ΉΧΙ ΈΡρ.
  - Theme guard test: math Ά░Α `style="value"` Ία╕Ή╢εΊΧαΈψΑΈκε `GEODE_THEME`
    ΉΩΡ Ά╖╕ style Ήκ┤Ήηυ verify έΑΦ PR #1165 Ήζα CRITICAL fix (`style="math"`
    Έψ╕ΉιΧΉζα crash) ΊγΝΆ╖Α Ή░ρΈΜρ.
- **CLI UI/UX regression tests for LaTeX rendering έΑΦ Stage A/B/C.**
  A three-stage regression suite anchored on the **user-visible CLI
  behaviour** so a future refactor of the rendering stack cannot
  silently regress the wiring that PR #1165 just shipped. 21 new tests
  in `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`.
  - **Stage A (Component capture, 9 cases)** drives
    `_render_text_with_latex` against a real `Rich.Console` writing into
    a `StringIO`, then asserts on plain-text substrings έΑΦ no raw
    delimiters left, expected Unicode characters present, prose
    boundaries preserved. Covers pure prose, `\[έΑο\]`, `\(έΑο\)`, `$x$`,
    the `$3.00` price false-positive guard, `\begin{equation}`,
    mixed segments, and text/math/text segment ordering.
  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrised cases)**
    asserts on **structural** properties of SymPy's `pretty()` output
    (substring group membership + minimum line count) for `\frac`,
    `\sum`, `\sqrt`, `\lim`, `\int`. Tolerates SymPy version drift
    (e.g. `έΦΑ` vs `-` for the fraction bar) by accepting a set of
    equivalent glyphs per slot. Zero snapshot brittleness.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 tests)** invokes
    `_render_ipc_response` with hand-crafted IPC dicts έΑΦ covers
    result + bracket math, math-free Markdown fallback, error
    responses, the streamed-vs-non-streamed tool fallback divergence,
    and silent drop of four lifecycle acks. Exercises the full
    `serve έΗΤ thin CLI` print path without an LLM in the loop.
  - Spinner thread leak avoidance (lesson from PR #1165's follow-up
    `time.sleep(0.08)` flake): every test uses a non-TTY console; no
    test starts `EventRenderer.start_activity()` or any other daemon
    animation, so a sibling test's `@patch("...time.sleep")` cannot
    accumulate the 80 ms spinner sleeps in its `mock.call_args_list`.
  - Theme guard test: math segments call `console.print(...,
    style="value")`. The test asserts that style is registered on
    `GEODE_THEME` so PR #1165's CRITICAL fix (Rich `MissingStyle`
    crash when `style="math"` was used) cannot regress.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX ΈιΝΈΞΦΈπΒ έΑΦ `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}έΑο\end{env}` delimiter Ή╢ΦΆ░Α.** PR #1141 Ήζ┤ `core/ui/latex.py`
  Ήζα Tier 1 (pylatexenc) + Tier 2 (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) Έζ╝Ήζ┤Έ╕Ν
  ΈθυΈου + 19 test ΈπΝ Ή╢ΦΆ░ΑΊΧαΆ│ι "ΈΜνΉζΝ ΈΜρΆ│Ε ΊδΕΈ│┤ έΑΦ event_renderer Ά░Α LLM ΉζΣΈΜ╡
  ΊΖΞΉΛνΊΛ╕ΉΩΡ extract_and_render_inline ΉιΒΉγσ" Ήε╝Έκε wiring ΉζΕ follow-up Ήε╝Έκε
  ΈΓρΆ▓ρΈΣΡΉΩΙΉζΝ. Ά▓░Ά│╝ΉιΒΉε╝Έκε ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡΈΛΦ LLM ΉζΣΈΜ╡ΉΩΡΉΕε `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}
  ^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` Ά░βΉζΑ raw LaTeX Έξ╝ Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε Έ│┤Ά│ι ΉηΙΉΩΙΈΜν. Έ│╕ PR Ήζ┤
  ΈΣΡ Ά░φΉζΕ ΈΜτΉζΝ:
  - `core/cli/interactive_loop.py` Ήζα `_render_ipc_response` Ά░Α LLM final
    text Έξ╝ `rich.markdown.Markdown` Ήε╝Έκε ΉπΒΉιΣ ΊζαΈουΈΞα Έ╢ΑΈ╢ΕΉζΕ ΉΜιΆ╖ε
    `_render_text_with_latex` ΊΩυΊΞ╝Έκε Ά╡ΡΉ▓┤. ΊΩυΊΞ╝ΈΛΦ
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)` Έκε segment Έ╢ΕΊΧι ΊδΕ inline math ΈΛΦ
    rendered Unicode Έκε Ήμ╝Έ│Α Markdown paragraph ΉΩΡ ΈΜνΉΜε ΊΧσΉ╣αΆ│ι,
    `block_math` ΈΛΦ multi-line block Ήε╝Έκε render. math Ά░Α ΉιΕΊαΑ ΉΩΗΉε╝Έσ┤
    ΈΜρΉζ╝ Markdown Ία╕Ή╢εΈκε fallback (ΊγΝΆ╖Α ΉεΕΊΩα 0).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` Ήζα delimiter Ά░Α `$...$` / `$$...$$` ΈΣΡ Ά░ΑΉπΑ Έ┐ΡΉζ┤Έζ╝
    LLM Ήζ┤ ΉηΡΉμ╝ Ή╢εΈιξΊΧαΈΛΦ `\[...\]` (display) / `\(...\)` (inline) /
    `\begin{equation|align|gather|multline|displaymath}έΑο\end{...}` Ά░Α
    ΈςρΈΣΡ ΈΙΕΈζ╜. Έ│╕ PR Ήζ┤ ΉΕ╕ ΊΝρΊΕ┤ ΈςρΈΣΡ ΉπΑΉδΡΊΧαΈΠΕΈκζ regex ΊβΧΉηξ + overlap-
    aware Ήγ░ΉΕιΉΙεΉεΕ resolution (block > inline) Ή╢ΦΆ░Α.
  - ΉΜιΆ╖ε test 13 (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` 7 +
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py` 6) έΑΦ ΈςρΈΥι delimiter form,
    mixed segments, overlap ΊγΝΊΦ╝, raw Έ░▒ΉΛυΈηαΉΜε leak ΊγΝΆ╖Α, ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡΆ░Α Έ│┤Ά│ιΊΧε
    `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` Ή╝ΑΉζ┤ΉΛν ΉπΒΉιΣ Ά▓ΑΉοζ.
  - ΉζαΈΠΕΈΡε Έ╣ΕΉπΑΉδΡ: backslash ΉΩΗΈΛΦ `[...]` / `(...)` έΑΦ markdown link
    Έυ╕Έ▓ΧΆ│╝ Ή╢σΈΠΝ + Ήζ╝Έ░α bracket ΉΨ┤Ίεα noise. ΉΓυΉγσΉηΡΈΛΦ `\[...\]` ΊαΧΉΜζΉζΕ ΉΞρΉΧ╝
    ΊΧρ.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering έΑΦ `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}έΑο\end{env}` delimiter support.** PR #1141 introduced
  `core/ui/latex.py` with the Tier 1 (pylatexenc) and Tier 2
  (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) renderers plus 19 tests, but the
  CHANGELOG flagged the actual wiring as a follow-up έΑΦ the response
  print path stayed on `rich.markdown.Markdown(text)`. Users therefore
  saw the raw backslash form (e.g. `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m}
  \ell(\alpha_i) \]`) in their terminals. This PR closes both gaps:
  - The LLM final-text branch of
    `core/cli/interactive_loop._render_ipc_response` now calls a new
    `_render_text_with_latex` helper. The helper splits the body via
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)`, folds inline math back into the
    surrounding Markdown paragraph as rendered Unicode, and renders
    `block_math` as a multi-line block. When the body has no math at
    all, it falls back to the single Markdown call (zero regression
    risk).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` only knew `$...$` and `$$...$$`. The new regex
    set adds the three forms LLMs actually emit έΑΦ `\[έΑο\]` for
    display, `\(έΑο\)` for inline, and `\begin{equation|align|gather|
    multline|displaymath}έΑο\end{...}` έΑΦ with overlap-aware priority
    resolution (block > inline) so an inline match inside a
    multi-line bracket block is not double-extracted.
  - 13 new tests
    (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` plus
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py`) pin every delimiter form,
    mixed segments, the overlap rule, the raw-backslash leak
    regression, and the user-reported case verbatim.
  - Deliberately not supported: bracket forms without backslashes
    (`[...]` / `(...)`) έΑΦ those collide with Markdown link syntax and
    ordinary parenthetical prose. Users must write `\[έΑο\]`.

## [0.95.1] έΑΦ 2026-05-16

### Infrastructure

- **`docs-link-audit` skill ΈΥ▒Έκζ.** `scripts/check_docs_links.py` (PR #1161)
  Έξ╝ 1Ή░ρ ΈΠΕΆ╡υΈκε ΊΧαΈΛΦ workflow skill ΉζΕ `.claude/skills/docs-link-audit/
  SKILL.md` ΉΩΡ Ή╢ΦΆ░Α. Έ╢ΕΈξα 4 ΉλΖ (internal /docs / internal /other / anchor
  / external) ΈπνΊΧΣ ΊΣε, link ΊΝρΊΕ┤ Ή╢ΦΉ╢ε ΉιΧΆ╖εΉΜζ 2 Ά░ε, ΊΛ╣Ήζ┤ Ή▓αΈου (`/geode/`
  basepath / build-time copy Ήζ╕ΉπΑ / `${...}` unresolved / ΉΛνΊΓ┤ ΉΛνΊΓ╡), exit
  code Ά╕░Έ░α CI guard, ΉηαΈς╗ΈΡε link Ήζα 4 ΊζΦΊΧε ΉδΡΉζ╕ (chapter ΉΓφΉιε leftover /
  section Ήζ┤ΉιΕ / slug typo / external rot), CI wiring Ήα╡ΉΖα 2 ΉλΖ (pages.yml
  pre-build / ci.yml dispatch) ΈςρΈΣΡ ΉιΧΈου. CLAUDE.md Ήζα Custom Skills ΊΣε
  ΉΩΡΈΠΕ ΊΛ╕ΈουΆ▒░ ΊΓνΉδΝΈΥε ("broken link", "404", "docs link", "hyperlink",
  "ΈπΒΊΒυ ΉιΡΆ▓Α", "ΈπΒΊΒυ Ά╣ρΉπΡ", "audit links", "link checker") ΈΥ▒Έκζ. PR
  #1157 (3 broken ΉιΧΉιΧ) + PR #1161 (script ΈΠΕΉηΖ) Ήζα Ή╝ΑΉζ┤ΉΛν ΉΛνΊΕ░ΈΦΦ ΊΠυΊΧρ.
- **`docs-link-audit` skill registered.** Added
  `.claude/skills/docs-link-audit/SKILL.md` as a workflow skill around
  `scripts/check_docs_links.py` (PR #1161). Covers the 4-category map
  (internal /docs / internal /other / anchor / external), the 2
  regexes that drive link extraction, special handling (`/geode/`
  basepath, build-time copy awareness, `${...}` as unresolved, scheme
  skip list), exit-code-based CI guard semantics, four common
  root causes of broken links (chapter deletion leftover, section
  move, slug typo, external rot), and two CI wiring options (pages.yml
  pre-build vs ci.yml dispatch). CLAUDE.md Custom Skills table now
  carries the trigger keywords ("broken link", "404", "docs link",
  "hyperlink", "ΈπΒΊΒυ ΉιΡΆ▓Α", "ΈπΒΊΒυ Ά╣ρΉπΡ", "audit links", "link
  checker"). Case studies from PR #1157 (3 broken corrected) + PR
  #1161 (script introduction) included.

- **`scripts/check_docs_links.py` έΑΦ docs ΉΓυΉζ┤ΊΛ╕ ΈπΒΊΒυ ΉιΧΉιΒ + HTTP ΉιΡΆ▓Α
  ΉΛνΊΒυΈο╜ΊΛ╕.** site/src Ήζα ΈςρΈΥι `.tsx`/`.ts` ΉΩΡΉΕε Έ│╕Έυ╕/JSX ΈπΒΊΒυ ΊΝρΊΕ┤ (
  `href="..."`, ``href={`...`}``, `src="..."`, `to="..."`, Ά╖╕ΈουΆ│ι markdown
  ΉΛνΊΔΑΉζ╝ ΈπΒΊΒυ ΊΣεΆ╕░) ΉζΕ ΈςρΈΣΡ Ή╢ΦΉ╢ε. 4 Έ╢ΕΈξα:
  - **internal /docs/...** έΑΦ `site/src/app/docs/` ΊΧαΉεΕ `page.tsx` slug
    ΉβΑ Ή░ρΉπΣΊΧσ έΗΤ ΈΙΕΈζ╜ ΉΜε broken
  - **internal /<other>...** έΑΦ `/portfolio`, `/works`, `/petri-bundle/`
    ΈΥ▒ έΗΤ app route + public asset + build-time copy (pages.yml Ήζα
    `docs/petri-bundle/` έΗΤ `site/out/petri-bundle/` step Ήζ╕ΉπΑ) ΉβΑ ΈΝΑΉκ░
  - **anchor #section** έΑΦ Ά░βΉζΑ page.tsx Ήζα `id="..."` ΉβΑ ΈΝΑΉκ░
  - **external http(s)://** έΑΦ `--http` Ήα╡ΊΛ╕Ήζ╕ ΉΜε HEAD/GET Ήε╝Έκε reachability
    Ά▓ΑΉΓυ (concurrent 8, 8s timeout, 200/3xx OK)
  CI ΊΗ╡ΊΧσ Ήα╡ΉΖα: `python3 scripts/check_docs_links.py` ΈπΝΉε╝Έκε ΉιΧΉιΒ Ά▓ΑΉΓυ
  ΊΗ╡Ά│╝ ΉΜε exit 0, broken Έ░εΆ▓υ ΉΜε exit 1. ΊΨξΊδΕ pages.yml build job Ήζα
  pre-build step ΈαΡΈΛΦ Έ│Ε ci.yml lint Ήε╝Έκε wiring Ά░ΑΈΛξ.

  ΊαΕΉηυ Ή╕κΉιΧ (Ήζ┤ PR ΉιΒΉγσ ΊδΕ): 193 link ΉΛνΉ║Φ, 0 broken, 17 external ΈςρΈΣΡ
  reachable, 2 unresolved (markdown-lite.tsx Ήζα regex ΊΝρΊΕ┤ Έυ╕ΉηΡΉΩ┤, false
  positive Έυ┤ΉΜε).
- **`scripts/check_docs_links.py` έΑΦ static + HTTP audit script for docs
  site links.** Extracts every link-shaped string from `site/src/**.tsx`
  / `.ts` (JSX `href="..."`, ``href={`...`}``, `src="..."`, `to="..."`,
  and markdown-style link syntax inside string literals). Classifies
  into four buckets:
  - **internal /docs/...** έΑΦ diffed against the `page.tsx` slug set
    under `site/src/app/docs/`; misses flagged.
  - **internal /\<other\>...** έΑΦ `/portfolio`, `/works`,
    `/petri-bundle/` etc., diffed against app routes + public assets +
    build-time copies (the script recognises the
    `docs/petri-bundle/` έΗΤ `site/out/petri-bundle/` copy step in
    `.github/workflows/pages.yml`).
  - **anchor #section** έΑΦ checked against `id="..."` occurrences in
    the emitting `page.tsx`.
  - **external http(s)://** έΑΦ opt-in `--http` HEAD/GET probe
    (concurrency 8, 8 s timeout, 200/3xx considered OK).
  Returns exit 0 on a clean run and exit 1 if anything is broken, so
  the script can later be wired into the Pages build (pre-build step)
  or into the `ci.yml` lint job. Current measurement after this PR:
  193 link occurrences scanned, 0 broken, all 17 external URLs
  reachable, 2 false-positive "unresolved" entries from regex pattern
  strings in `markdown-lite.tsx` (informational, not failure).

### Fixed

- **Docs ΉΓυΉζ┤ΊΛ╕ broken link 3 Ά░ε ΉιΧΉιΧ (6 ΉΓυΉζ┤ΊΛ╕).** docs ΉΓυΉζ┤ΊΛ╕ ΈΓ┤Έ╢Α ΈπΒΊΒυ
  ΉιΧΉιΒ ΉΛνΉ║Φ Ά▓░Ά│╝ ΈΜνΉζΝ 3 Ά▓╜ΈκεΆ░Α 404 ΉαΑΉζΝ έΑΦ ΊΧ┤ΈΜ╣ ΊΟαΉζ┤ΉπΑΆ░Α sitemap ΉΩΡ Ήκ┤ΉηυΊΧαΉπΑ
  ΉΧΛΆ▒░ΈΓα ΈΜνΈξ╕ slug Έκε Ήζ┤ΉιΕΈΡε ΉΔΒΊΔε:
  - `/docs/build/add-domain` έΗΤ `/docs/runtime/domains` (D ΉΛνΊΦΕΈο░ΊΛ╕ΉΩΡΉΕε
    `build/` Ή▒ΧΊΕ░ ΉΓφΉιε ΊδΕ ΈΓρΉζΑ leftover Ή░╕Ήκ░ 2 ΉΓυΉζ┤ΊΛ╕ έΑΦ `run/analyze/page.tsx`
    L38, L65). ΉΜνΉιε ΈΠΕΈσΦΉζ╕ Ή╢ΦΆ░Α Έυ╕ΉΕεΈΛΦ `runtime/domains` ΉΛυΈθυΆ╖╕.
  - `/docs/build/add-tool` έΗΤ `/docs/runtime/tools/protocol` (`run/messaging/
    page.tsx` L35, L60). ΈΠΕΆ╡υ ΊΦΕΈκεΊΗιΉ╜ε Έυ╕ΉΕεΈΛΦ `runtime/tools/protocol` ΉΛυΈθυΆ╖╕.
  - `/docs/ops/observability` έΗΤ `/docs/verification/observability`
    (`petri/run/page.tsx` L77, L146). Ά┤ΑΉ╕κΉΕ▒ Έυ╕ΉΕεΈΛΦ `ops/` Ά░Α ΉΧΕΈΜΙΈζ╝
    `verification/` ΉΕ╣ΉΖα ΊΧαΉεΕ ΉΛυΈθυΆ╖╕.

  ΊΔΡΉπΑ Έ░σΈ▓Χ έΑΦ `grep` Ήε╝Έκε `site/src/` Ήζα ΈςρΈΥι `href="(/docs/...)"`,
  `href={\`/docs/...\`}`, markdown style `](/docs/...)` ΊΝρΊΕ┤ 23 Ά░ε Ή╢ΦΉ╢ε έΗΤ
  `find site/src/app/docs -name "page.tsx"` Ήζα 50 Ά░ε ΉΜνΉηυ ΊΟαΉζ┤ΉπΑ ΉΛυΈθυΆ╖╕ΉβΑ
  `comm -23` Ήε╝Έκε Ή░ρΉπΣΊΧσ έΗΤ 3 broken Έ░εΆ▓υ. `npm run build` ΉΕ▒Ά│╡ ΊδΕ 6 ΉΓυΉζ┤ΊΛ╕
  Ά╡ΡΉ▓┤. doc Έ│ΑΆ▓╜ only, ΊΨΚΉεΕ Έ│ΑΆ▓╜ 0.

- **Docs site broken-link fix έΑΦ 3 paths (6 sites).** A static scan of
  internal links in `site/src/` found three routes that returned 404
  because the target slug either no longer exists (an earlier `build/`
  chapter was retired) or was renamed to another section:
  - `/docs/build/add-domain` έΗΤ `/docs/runtime/domains`
    (`run/analyze/page.tsx` L38, L65).
  - `/docs/build/add-tool` έΗΤ `/docs/runtime/tools/protocol`
    (`run/messaging/page.tsx` L35, L60).
  - `/docs/ops/observability` έΗΤ `/docs/verification/observability`
    (`petri/run/page.tsx` L77, L146).

  Detection used `grep` against `site/src/` for all `href="(/docs/...)"`,
  ``href={`/docs/...`}``, and markdown-style `](/docs/...)` patterns
  (23 unique link targets) cross-referenced via `comm -23` against the
  50 page-slug paths under `site/src/app/docs/**/page.tsx`; the
  difference produced exactly 3 broken targets. `npm run build` passes
  after the 6 edits. Documentation only έΑΦ no behavioural impact.

### Added

- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py` Ήζα `assemble()` ΉΩΡ Phase 0 (Wrapper
  Override) Ή╢ΦΆ░Α. env var `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` Ά░Α set
  ΈΡαΈσ┤ JSON ΉζΕ `dict[str, str]` Έκε ΈκεΈΥεΊΧ┤ Ά╖╕ value ΈΥνΉζΕ concat ΊΧε Ά▓░Ά│╝Έκε
  `base_system` ΉζΕ ΈΝΑΉ▓┤. ΊδΕΉΗΞ Phase (skill / memory / extra) ΈΛΦ Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε
  ΉιΒΉγσ. env unset ΉζΑ baseline ΉζΕ ΉειΉπΑΊΧαΉπΑΈπΝ, env Ά░Α set ΈΡε ΈΤν ΊΝΝΉζ╝ ΈΙΕΈζ╜ /
  malformed JSON / dict ΉΧΕΈΜα / empty dict / non-string entry Ά░Α ΈΓαΉανΈσ┤
  fail-closed `RuntimeError` Έκε real audit quota Έξ╝ baseline prompt ΉΩΡ
  ΉΥ░ΉπΑ ΉΧΛΆ▓Ν ΊΧρ. `autoresearch/train.py` Ήζα
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY` Έξ╝ `True` Έκε flip ΊΧ┤ real-mode ΊβεΉΕ▒ΊβΦ έΑΦ
  outer-loop agent Ά░Α `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` Έξ╝ ΉΙαΉιΧΊΧαΈσ┤ `geode audit`
  Ήζα system prompt Ά░Α ΉΜνΉιεΈκε Ά╖╕ dict Ήζα ΈΓ┤ΉγσΉε╝Έκε ΈΠβΉηΣ. `.env.example` ΉΩΡ
  `# GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=` ΊΧφΈςσ + ΉΓυΉγσ ΉΕνΈςΖ Ή╢ΦΆ░Α. ΉΜιΆ╖ε 9 pytest
  (`tests/test_prompt_assembler.py` Ήζα `TestWrapperOverrideHook` έΑΦ
  env-unset baseline / ΉιΧΉΔΒ override / ΊΝΝΉζ╝ ΈΙΕΈζ╜ raise / malformed
  JSON raise / Έ╣Ε-dict raise / empty dict raise / non-string entry raise /
  hash Ά┤ΑΉ╕κΉΕ▒ / extra ΊΧσΉΕ▒)
  + train.py Ήζα fail-fast test Έξ╝ real-mode subprocess argv/env Ά▓ΑΉοζ
  Ήε╝Έκε Ά╡ΡΉ▓┤ (mock subprocess, quota ΉΓυΉγσ ΉΩΗΉζΝ).
- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  Adds Phase 0 (Wrapper Override) to `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py`'s
  `assemble()`. When `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` is set, the
  JSON is loaded as `dict[str, str]` and its values are concatenated to
  replace `base_system`; the remaining phases (skill / memory / extra)
  still apply on top. When the env is unset, baseline behavior is
  unchanged; once the env is set, missing files, malformed JSON,
  non-dict payloads, empty dicts, or non-string entries fail closed with
  `RuntimeError` so real audit quota is not spent on the baseline prompt.
  `autoresearch/train.py` flips
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY = True`, enabling real-mode runs έΑΦ the
  outer-loop agent's edits to `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` now actually
  reach the `geode audit` system prompt. `.env.example` documents the
  new optional variable. Nine new pytest cases in
  `tests/test_prompt_assembler.py::TestWrapperOverrideHook` (baseline /
  override / missing file raises / malformed JSON raises / non-dict raises /
  empty dict raises / non-string entries raise / hash observability /
  composition with bootstrap extras) plus
  the existing `tests/test_autoresearch_train.py` fail-fast test
  replaced by a real-mode subprocess argv/env assertion (subprocess
  mocked έΑΦ no LLM quota consumed).

- **Phase 1a έΑΦ Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** Hermes
  ΊζκΉΙα plan(`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-absorption.md`) Ήζα Ή▓τ
  PR. `sessions.db` ΉΩΡ `messages` ΊΖΝΉζ┤Έ╕Φ (id / session_id / seq / role /
  content / tool_call_id / tool_calls / tool_name / timestamp /
  token_count / finish_reason / reasoning / metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id,
  seq)`) + `idx_messages_session` + `idx_messages_tool_name` ΉΜιΉΕν.
  `SessionCheckpoint.save()` Ά░Α JSON Έ│╕Έυ╕ ΉιΑΉηξ ΉπΒΊδΕ
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()` Έκε Έ│╕Έυ╕ΉζΕ mirror έΑΦ JSON ΉζΑ Phase 1b
  Ήζα SoT ΉιΕΊβαΆ╣ΝΉπΑ authoritative. DB ΉΜνΊΝρ ΉΜε WARN ΈκεΆ╣Ζ + `exc_info=True`,
  JSON Έ│╕Έυ╕ΉζΑ Ά╖╕ΈΝΑΈκε Έ│┤Ήκ┤ (graceful degradation). ΈΠβΉζ╝/Ή╢ΧΉΗΝ/Έ╣Ι message
  list Ήζα ΉηυΉιΑΉηξ ΈςρΈΣΡ idempotent έΑΦ ΉνΕΉΨ┤ΈΥι seq Ήζα stale row ΉβΑ Έ╣Ι ΉιΑΉηξΉζα
  ΉηΦΉΩυ row Ά╣ΝΉπΑ ΉιεΆ▒░ΊΧ┤ JSON έΗΦ DB Ά░Α ΊΧφΉΔΒ ΉιΧΈιυ. Anthropic content blocks
  (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) ΉβΑ OpenAI ΊαΧΉΜζ (`tool_calls`
  / `tool_call_id` / `name`) ΉΨΣΉς╜ Ή╢ΦΉ╢ε + 18 ΉΜιΆ╖ε ΊΖΝΉΛνΊΛ╕ (dual-write
  parity / sqlite ΉΜνΊΝρ graceful / openai+anthropic Ή╢ΦΉ╢ε / stale row
  ΉιεΆ▒░ / Έ╣Ι ΉιΑΉηξ ΉιΧΊΧσ). Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier Ά░Α CRITICAL 2 Ά▒┤
  (stale row + Έ╣Ι-save ΉηΦΉηυ) ΉζΕ Έ░εΆ▓υ┬╖Έ░αΉαΒ.
- **Phase 1a έΑΦ Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** First PR
  of the Hermes-absorption plan (`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-
  absorption.md`). Adds a `messages` table to `sessions.db` (columns: id
  / session_id / seq / role / content / tool_call_id / tool_calls /
  tool_name / timestamp / token_count / finish_reason / reasoning /
  metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id, seq)`) plus `idx_messages_session` and
  `idx_messages_tool_name`. `SessionCheckpoint.save()` mirrors the full
  message list into the table right after the JSON write, via
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()`; JSON remains SoT until Phase 1b
  flips the source. DB failures emit a WARNING with `exc_info=True` and
  leave the JSON checkpoint intact (graceful degradation). Re-saving the
  same, shorter, or empty message list is idempotent έΑΦ stale rows from a
  shrunk transcript and leftovers from an empty save are removed so JSON
  and the mirror stay aligned. The extractor reads both Anthropic content
  blocks (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) and OpenAI-style
  fields (`tool_calls` / `tool_call_id` / `name`). 18 new tests cover
  dual-write parity, a real `sqlite3.OperationalError` graceful path,
  OpenAI/Anthropic extraction, stale-row removal, and empty-save
  alignment. A Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier round caught two CRITICAL
  gaps (stale rows on shrink, leftovers on empty save), both fixed.

### Changed

- **Autoresearch outer-loop: Karpathy 3-file fork (Petri-signal domain).**
  PR #1145 Ήζα 6-module python stub (`loop.py` / `hypothesis.py` /
  `fitness.py` / `ratchet.py` / `rationale_extractor.py` /
  `baseline_marker.py`, 480 LOC, "follow-up PR1" ΊΣεΆ╕░Έκε implementation
  ΈΝΑΆ╕░) ΉζΕ ΊΠΡΆ╕░ΊΧαΆ│ι Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) (228791f, MIT,
  26K+ stars) Ήζα 3-file ΊΝρΊΕ┤ (`prepare.py` + `train.py` + `program.md`)
  Ήε╝Έκε ΉηυΆ╡υΉΕ▒. ML ΈΠΕΈσΦΉζ╕ (GPT pre-train + `val_bpb`) ΉζΕ GEODE alignment-
  audit ΈΠΕΈσΦΉζ╕ (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval 5-axis fitness) Ήε╝Έκε Ή╡εΉΗΝ
  Ά╡ΡΉ▓┤. Karpathy ΉδΡΈ│╕Ήζα single-mutation-file + fixed-budget + grep-friendly
  stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col + git-as-optimizer ΉιΧΉΜι ΉειΉπΑ.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` έΑΦ `~/.cache/autoresearch/` Ήζα fineweb ΈΜνΉγ┤
    ΈκεΈΥε + tokenizer ΊΧβΉΛ╡ ΉηΡΈουΉΩΡ **fixed audit harness sanity check**
    (10 safe seed file count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit
    --help` reachability). `~/.cache` Ά░Α read-only Ήζ╝ ΈΧΝ worktree-local
    fallback.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` έΑΦ GPT ΊΧβΉΛ╡ ΉηΡΈουΉΩΡ **wrapper system-prompt
    section dict** (mutation target) + `geode audit` subprocess invocation
    (staged: `--seed-select` / `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) + 5-axis
    fitness extraction. ΊαΕΉηυ GEODE core Ά░Α `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Έξ╝
    ΉΧΕΉπΒ consume ΊΧαΉπΑ ΉΧΛΉε╝ΈψΑΈκε real mode ΈΛΦ fail-fast (`RuntimeError`),
    `--dry-run` ΈπΝ working mode έΑΦ runtime hook ΉζΑ follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` έΑΦ ML research direction ΉηΡΈουΉΩΡ Petri
    direction (gen 0 hypothesis space, `input_hallucination` ╬Φ +1.13 /
    `overrefusal` +0.31 / `broken_tool_use` έΙΤ1.28 driver seed Ήζα prior,
    9 hypothesis = wrapper section ablation).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` έΑΦ Petri-signal fork Ήζα quick start.
  - `pyproject.toml` Ήζα `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point ΉιεΆ▒░ (Karpathy ΉδΡΈ│╕ ΉιΧΉΜι: single-script `uv run python
    autoresearch/train.py`, CLI wrapping ΉΩΗΉζΝ).
  - 3 ΉΜιΆ╖ε pytest (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) έΑΦ argv Ά░Α ΊαΕΉηυ
    `geode audit` flag ΈπΝ ΉΓυΉγσ + ΉΓυΈζ╝ΉπΕ flag Ήζα retro ΊγΝΊΦ╝ + real-mode
    fail-fast + dry-run baseline Ήζα fitness range Ά▓ΑΉοζ.
  - Ά╖╕Έζ╝Ήγ┤ΈΦσ reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (ΈΜρΉΙε clone,
    GEODE repo ΉβΑ git ΉΩ░ΈΠβ ΉΩΗΉζΝ).
  - Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier Ά░Α CRITICAL 1 + HIGH 4 ΉηΡΈΠβ fix έΑΦ
    real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flag, rubric grep Ήζα stale ΊαΧΉΜζ,
    `~/.cache` Ά╢ΝΊΧε fallback.
- **Autoresearch outer-loop: Karpathy 3-file fork (Petri-signal domain).**
  Retires the 6-module Python stub from PR #1145 (`loop.py` /
  `hypothesis.py` / `fitness.py` / `ratchet.py` / `rationale_extractor.py`
  / `baseline_marker.py`, 480 LOC, "follow-up PR1" placeholder) and
  rebases the outer loop on the Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) 3-file pattern
  (228791f, MIT, 26K+ stars). Domain swapped from ML pre-training
  (`val_bpb`) to GEODE alignment-audit (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval
  5-axis fitness), preserving Karpathy's single-mutation-file +
  fixed-budget + grep-friendly stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col +
  git-as-optimizer spirit.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` replaces fineweb download + tokenizer
    training with a **fixed audit harness sanity check** (seed file
    count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit --help` reachability),
    and falls back to a worktree-local report if `~/.cache` is read-only.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` replaces the GPT training loop with a
    **wrapper system-prompt section dict** (the agent's mutation surface)
    plus a `geode audit` subprocess invocation (staged: `--seed-select` /
    `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) and 5-axis fitness extraction.
    Until GEODE core consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`, real mode
    fail-fasts and only `--dry-run` is non-deceptive; the runtime hook
    lands in a follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` replaces the ML research direction with a
    Petri direction (gen-0 hypothesis space drawn from yesterday's
    driver seeds: `input_hallucination` ╬Φ +1.13 / `overrefusal` +0.31 /
    `broken_tool_use` έΙΤ1.28 across three model families, pruning the
    space to nine wrapper-section ablations).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` ships a Petri-signal fork quick-start.
  - `pyproject.toml` drops the `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point έΑΦ Karpathy keeps the runner as a plain `python train.py`,
    so the fork does too.
  - Three new pytest tests (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) pin the
    current `geode audit` flag set, block obsolete flags from creeping
    back in, assert the real-mode fail-fast, and check the dry-run
    fitness range.
  - Grounding reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (a plain
    clone, not wired into the GEODE repo).
  - Codex MCP acted as a cross-LLM verifier and applied one CRITICAL fix
    plus four HIGH fixes έΑΦ real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flags,
    stale rubric grep, and the `~/.cache` permission fallback.

### Fixed

- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold verifier fixes.** `prepare.py` now parses the
  19-dimension YAML rubric instead of grepping for a stale `- name:` shape,
  falls back to a workspace-local prepare report when `~/.cache` is not
  writable, and `train.py` fail-fast blocks real audit mode until GEODE core
  actually consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`. The staged live argv now matches
  the current `geode audit` CLI (`--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`,
  `--yes`) instead of obsolete `--rubric` / `--budget-minutes` flags.
- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold Ά▓ΑΉοζ ΉΙαΉιΧ.** `prepare.py` Ά░Α ΉανΈηαΈΡε
  `- name:` ΊαΧΉΜζ grep ΈΝΑΉΜι 19-dim YAML rubric ΉζΕ ΉπΒΉιΣ parse ΊΧαΆ│ι,
  `~/.cache` ΉΩΡ ΉΥ╕ ΉΙα ΉΩΗΉζΕ ΈΧΝ worktree-local prepare report Έκε fallback
  ΊΧσΈΜΙΈΜν. `train.py` ΈΛΦ GEODE core Ά░Α `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Έξ╝ ΉΜνΉιεΈκε
  consume ΊΧαΆ╕░ ΉιΕΆ╣ΝΉπΑ real audit mode Έξ╝ fail-fast Έκε ΈπΚΉΧΕ, wrapper mutation
  Ήζ┤ ΉιΒΉγσΈΡαΈΛΦ Ά▓ΔΉ▓αΈθ╝ Έ│┤Ήζ┤ΈΛΦ Ή░σΉΜεΈξ╝ ΉιεΆ▒░ΊΨΙΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν. staged live argv ΈΠΕ ΊαΕΉηυ
  `geode audit` CLI Ήζα `--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`, `--yes` ΉΩΡ
  ΈπηΉ╖ΕΉΛ╡ΈΜΙΈΜν.

  200+έΗΤ200, module 353έΗΤ363, test 4608έΗΤ4897.** ΉπΒΉιΕ unified-daemon
  ΈΜνΉζ┤ΉΨ┤Ά╖╕Έηρ self-audit ΉΩΡΉΕε Έ░εΆ▓υΈΡε outdated ΉΙαΉ╣α ΉιΧΉιΧ. README/README.ko
  Ήζα (a) shields.io Έ░░ΉπΑ, (b) `What's inside` ΊΣε, (c) peer comparison ΊΣε
  Ήζα MCP ΉΖΑ, (d) Architecture overview Ήζα `Runtime Tools(N)` /
  `ToolRegistry(N)` / `Skills(N)` Έζ╝Έ▓ρ, (e) `GEODE Runtime` ΈΜρΈζ╜Ήζα ΈΠΕΆ╡υ
  / Skill Ή╣┤Ήγ┤ΊΛ╕ ΈςρΈΣΡ ΉΜνΉ╕κΆ░ΤΉε╝Έκε Ά░▒ΉΜι. CLAUDE.md Ήζα `Modules` (`find
  core/ -name "*.py" \| wc -l` = 318, `plugins/` = 45) + `Tests` (`pytest
  --collect-only -m "not live"` = 4897) Ή╣┤Ήγ┤ΊΛ╕ΈΠΕ ΈΠβΆ╕░ΊβΦ. Ή╕κΉιΧ Έ░σΉΜζ: (1)
  `core/tools/definitions.json` JSON Ά╕╕Ήζ┤ = 61. (2) `SkillLoader(lazy=
  True).load_all()` Ά╕╕Ήζ┤ = 14 (bundled+global+project ΉΛνΉ╜ΦΊΦΕ ΊΧσΉΓ░).
  (3) `~/.geode/mcp/registry-cache.json` Ήζα `servers` array Ά╕╕Ήζ┤ =
  ΉιΧΊβΧΊηΙ 200 (ΉαΙΉιΕ "200+" ΈΛΦ Έ╢ΑΉιΧΊβΧ). ΊΨΚΉεΕ Έ│ΑΆ▓╜ 0 έΑΦ doc ΉΙαΉ╣α only.
- **README + CLAUDE.md count grounding έΑΦ tool 25έΗΤ61, skill 13έΗΤ14,
  MCP 200+έΗΤ200, module 353έΗΤ363, test 4608έΗΤ4897.** Outdated counts
  discovered while self-auditing the unified-daemon diagram were
  resynced against measured values. Updated in README and README.ko:
  (a) shields.io badges, (b) `What's inside` table, (c) peer
  comparison MCP cell, (d) `Tools(N)` / `ToolRegistry(N)` /
  `Skills(N)` labels in the Architecture overview, (e) `GEODE
  Runtime` paragraph tool / skill counts. CLAUDE.md `Modules` and

  `core/tools/definitions.json` JSON array = 61. (2)
  `SkillLoader(lazy=True).load_all()` returns 14 across
  bundled/global/project scopes. (3) `~/.geode/mcp/registry-cache.
